  [inst_3 : AddCommGroup V₂] [inst_4 : Module K V₂] [FiniteDimensional K V] (f : V →ₗ[K] V₂),
  Module.finrank K ↥f.range + Module.finrank K ↥f.ker = Module.finrank K V"},{"name":"LinearMap.rank_range_add_rank_ker","module_name":"Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Dimension.RankNullity","kind":"theorem","type":"∀ {R : Type u_1} {M M₁ : Type u} [inst : Ring R] [inst_1 : AddCommGroup M] [inst_2 : AddCommGroup M₁]
  [inst_3 : Module R M] [inst_4 : Module R M₁] [HasRankNullity.{u, u_1} R] (f : M →ₗ[R] M₁),
  Module.rank R ↥f.range + Module.rank R ↥f.ker = Module.rank R M"},{"name":"LinearMap.rank_le_of_surjective","module_name":"Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Dimension.Basic","kind":"theorem","type":"∀ {R : Type u} {M M₁ : Type v} [inst : Semiring R] [inst_1 : AddCommMonoid M] [inst_2 : Module R M]
  [inst_3 : AddCommMonoid M₁] [inst_4 : Module R M₁] (f : M →ₗ[R] M₁),
  Function.Surjective ⇑f → Module.rank R M₁ ≤ Module.rank R M"}]}

{"items":[{"name":"Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_encard","kind":"lemma","file":".lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Module/SpanRank.lean"},{"name":"Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite","kind":"lemma","file":".lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Module/SpanRank.lean"}]}

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2025 Andrew Yang. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Wanyi He, Jiedong Jiang, Xuchun Li, Christian Merten, Jingting Wang, Andrew Yang
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Data.ENat.Lattice
9	public import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Dimension.Free
10	public import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Dimension.StrongRankCondition
11	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Finiteness.Ideal
12	
13	/-!
14	# Minimum Cardinality of generating set of a submodule
15	
16	In this file, we define the minimum cardinality of a generating set for a submodule, which is
17	implemented as `spanFinrank` and `spanRank`.
18	`spanFinrank` takes value in `ℕ` and equals `0` when no finite generating set exists.
19	`spanRank` takes value as a cardinal.
20	
21	## Main Definitions
22	
23	* `spanFinrank`: The minimum cardinality of a generating set of a submodule as a natural
24	  number. If no finite generating set exists, it is defined to be `0`.
25	* `spanRank`: The minimum cardinality of a generating set of a submodule as a cardinal.
26	* `FG.generators`: For a finitely generated submodule, get a set of generating elements with minimal
27	  cardinality.
28	
29	## Main Results
30	
31	* `FG.exists_span_set_card_eq_spanFinrank` : Any submodule has a generating set of cardinality equal
32	  to `spanRank`.
33	
34	* `rank_eq_spanRank_of_free` : For a ring `R` (not necessarily commutative) satisfying
35	  `StrongRankCondition R`, if `M` is a free `R`-module, then the `spanRank` of `M` equals to the
36	  rank of M.
37	
38	* `rank_le_spanRank` : For a ring `R` (not necessarily commutative) satisfying
39	  `StrongRankCondition R`, if `M` is an `R`-module, then the `spanRank` of `M` is less than or equal
40	  to the rank of M.
41	
42	## Tags
43	submodule, generating subset, span rank
44	
45	## Remark
46	Note that the corresponding API - `Module.rank` is only defined for a module rather than a
47	submodule, so there is some asymmetry here. Further refactoring might be needed if this difference
48	creates a friction later on.
49	-/
50	
51	@[expose] public section
52	
53	namespace Submodule
54	
55	section Defs
56	
57	universe u v
58	
59	variable {R : Type*} {M : Type u} [Semiring R] [AddCommMonoid M] [Module R M]
60	
61	open Cardinal
62	
63	/-- The minimum cardinality of a generating set of a submodule as a cardinal. -/
64	noncomputable def spanRank (p : Submodule R M) : Cardinal := ⨅ (s : {s : Set M // span R s = p}), #s
65	
66	/-- The minimum cardinality of a generating set of a submodule as a natural number. If no finite
67	  generating set exists, the span rank is defined to be `0`. -/
68	noncomputable def spanFinrank (p : Submodule R M) : ℕ := (spanRank p).toNat
69	
70	instance (p : Submodule R M) : Nonempty {s : Set M // span R s = p} := ⟨⟨p, by simp⟩⟩
71	
72	lemma spanRank_toENat_eq_iInf_encard (p : Submodule R M) : p.spanRank.toENat =
73	    (⨅ (s : Set M) (_ : span R s = p), s.encard) := by
74	  rw [spanRank]
75	  apply le_antisymm
76	  · refine le_iInf₂ (fun s hs ↦ ?_)
77	    rw [Set.encard, ENat.card]
78	    exact toENat.monotone' (ciInf_le' _ (⟨s, hs⟩ : {s : Set M // span R s = p}))
79	  · have := congrFun toENat_comp_ofENat.{u}.symm (⨅ (s : Set M) (_ : span R s = p), s.encard)
80	    rw [id_eq] at this; rw [this]
81	    refine toENat.monotone' (le_ciInf fun s ↦ ?_)
82	    have : ofENat.{u} (⨅ (s' : Set M), ⨅ (_ : span R s' = p), s'.encard) ≤ ofENat s.1.encard :=
83	      ofENatHom.monotone' (le_trans (ciInf_le' _ s.1) (ciInf_le' _ s.2))
84	    apply le_trans this
85	    rw [Set.encard, ENat.card]
86	    exact Cardinal.ofENat_toENat_le _
87	
88	lemma spanRank_toENat_eq_iInf_finset_card (p : Submodule R M) :
89	    p.spanRank.toENat = ⨅ (s : {s : Finset M // span R s = p}), (s.1.card : ℕ∞) := by
90	  rw [spanRank_toENat_eq_iInf_encard]
91	  rcases eq_or_ne (⨅ (s : Set M) (_ : span R s = p), s.encard) ⊤ with (h1 | h2)
92	  · rw [h1, eq_comm]; simp_rw [iInf_eq_top] at h1 ⊢
93	    exact fun s ↦ False.elim (Set.encard_ne_top_iff.mpr s.1.finite_toSet (h1 s.1 s.2))
94	  · simp_rw [← Set.encard_coe_eq_coe_finsetCard]
95	    apply le_antisymm
96	    · exact le_iInf fun s ↦ iInf₂_le (s.1 : Set M) s.2
97	    · refine le_iInf fun s ↦ le_iInf fun h ↦ ?_
98	      by_cases hs : s.Finite
99	      · exact iInf_le_of_le ⟨hs.toFinset, by simpa⟩ (by simp)
100	      · rw [Set.Infinite.encard_eq hs]
101	        exact OrderTop.le_top _
102	
103	lemma spanFinrank_eq_iInf (p : Submodule R M) :
104	    p.spanFinrank = ⨅ (s : {s : Finset M // span R s = p}), s.1.card := by
105	  simp [spanFinrank, Cardinal.toNat, spanRank_toENat_eq_iInf_finset_card, ENat.iInf_toNat]
106	
107	/-- A submodule's `spanRank` is finite if and only if it is finitely generated. -/
108	@[simp]
109	lemma spanRank_finite_iff_fg {p : Submodule R M} : p.spanRank < aleph0 ↔ p.FG := by
110	  rw [spanRank, Submodule.fg_def]
111	  constructor
112	  · rintro h
113	    obtain ⟨s, hs⟩ : ⨅ (s : {s : Set M // span R s = p}), #s ∈
114	      Set.range (fun (s : {s : Set M // span R s = p}) ↦ #s) := csInf_mem ⟨#p, ⟨⟨p, by simp⟩, rfl⟩⟩
115	    refine ⟨s.1, ?_, s.2⟩
116	    simpa [← hs] using h
117	  · rintro ⟨s, hs₁, hs₂⟩
118	    exact (ciInf_le' _ ⟨s, hs₂⟩).trans_lt (by simpa)
119	
120	lemma spanFinrank_of_not_fg {p : Submodule R M} (hp : ¬p.FG) : p.spanFinrank = 0 := by
121	  refine toNat_eq_zero.2 ?_
122	  right
123	  by_contra! h
124	  exact hp (spanRank_finite_iff_fg.1 h)
125	
126	/-- A submodule is finitely generated if and only if its `spanRank` is equal to its `spanFinrank`.
127	-/
128	lemma fg_iff_spanRank_eq_spanFinrank {p : Submodule R M} : p.spanRank = p.spanFinrank ↔ p.FG := by
129	  rw [spanFinrank, ← spanRank_finite_iff_fg, eq_comm]
130	  exact cast_toNat_eq_iff_lt_aleph0
131	
132	lemma FG.spanRank_eq_spanFinrank {p : Submodule R M} (fg : p.FG) : p.spanRank = p.spanFinrank :=
133	  fg_iff_spanRank_eq_spanFinrank.mpr fg
134	
135	lemma FG.spanRank_le_iff {p : Submodule R M} (hp : p.FG) (n : ℕ) :
136	    p.spanRank ≤ n ↔ p.spanFinrank ≤ n :=
137	  (Cardinal.toNat_le_iff_of_lt_aleph0 n (by simpa)).symm
138	
139	lemma FG.spanRank_eq_iff {p : Submodule R M} (hp : p.FG) (n : ℕ) :
140	    p.spanRank = n ↔ p.spanFinrank = n :=
141	  (Cardinal.toNat_eq_iff_of_lt_aleph0 n (by simpa)).symm
142	
143	lemma spanRank_span_le_card (s : Set M) : (Submodule.span R s).spanRank ≤ #s := by
144	  rw [spanRank]
145	  let s' : {s1 : Set M // span R s1 = span R s} := ⟨s, rfl⟩
146	  exact ciInf_le' _ s'
147	
148	lemma spanRank_span_range_of_linearIndependent [RankCondition R] {ι : Type u} {v : ι → M}
149	    (hv : v.Injective) (hs : LinearIndependent R v) :
150	    (span R (.range v)).spanRank = #ι := by
151	  refine le_antisymm (le_trans (spanRank_span_le_card _) mk_range_le) (le_ciInf fun x ↦ ?_)
152	  have : #x.1 = #((Subtype.val : span R (.range v) → _) ⁻¹' x.1) :=
153	    (mk_preimage_of_injective_of_subset_range _ _ Subtype.val_injective (by simp [← x.2])).symm
154	  rw [this]
155	  refine le_trans ?_ ((Module.Basis.span hs).le_span (R := R) (J := Subtype.val ⁻¹' x.1) ?_)
156	  · rw [mk_range_eq]
157	    exact .of_comp (f := Subtype.val) (by convert hv; ext; simp [Module.Basis.span_apply])
158	  · apply map_injective_of_injective (f := (span R _).subtype) (injective_subtype _)
159	    simp [map_span, Set.image_preimage_eq_inter_range, Set.inter_eq_self_of_subset_left, ← x.2]
160	
161	lemma spanRank_span_of_linearIndepOn [RankCondition R] (s : Set M) (hs : LinearIndepOn R id s) :
162	    (span R s).spanRank = #s := by
163	  simp [← spanRank_span_range_of_linearIndependent Subtype.val_injective hs]
164	
165	lemma spanFinrank_span_le_encard (s : Set M) : (span R s).spanFinrank ≤ s.encard := by
166	  rw [spanFinrank, Set.encard, ENat.card]
167	  exact le_trans (by simp) (toENat.monotone' (spanRank_span_le_card (R := R) s))
168	
169	lemma spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite {s : Set M} (hs : s.Finite) :
170	    (span R s).spanFinrank ≤ s.ncard := by
171	  rw [← Nat.cast_le (α := ℕ∞)]
172	  exact le_trans (spanFinrank_span_le_encard _) hs.cast_ncard_eq.ge
173	
174	/-- Constructs a generating set with cardinality equal to the `spanRank` of the submodule -/
175	theorem exists_span_set_card_eq_spanRank (p : Submodule R M) :
176	    ∃ s : Set M, #s = p.spanRank ∧ span R s = p := by
177	  rw [spanRank]
178	  obtain ⟨s, hs⟩ : ⨅ (s : {s : Set M // span R s = p}), #s ∈
179	    Set.range (fun (s : {s : Set M // span R s = p}) ↦ #s) := csInf_mem ⟨#p, ⟨⟨p, by simp⟩, rfl⟩⟩
180	  exact ⟨s.1, ⟨hs, s.2⟩⟩
181	
182	/-- Constructs a generating set with cardinality equal to the `spanFinrank` of the submodule when
183	  the submodule is finitely generated. -/
184	theorem FG.exists_span_set_encard_eq_spanFinrank {p : Submodule R M} (h : p.FG) :
185	    ∃ s : Set M, s.encard = p.spanFinrank ∧ span R s = p := by
186	  obtain ⟨s, ⟨hs₁, hs₂⟩⟩ := exists_span_set_card_eq_spanRank p
187	  refine ⟨s, ⟨?_, hs₂⟩⟩
188	  have := fg_iff_spanRank_eq_spanFinrank.mpr h
189	  rw [Set.encard, ENat.card, spanFinrank, hs₁, this]
190	  simp
191	
192	/-- Constructs a generating finset with cardinality equal to the `spanFinrank` of the submodule
193	  when the submodule is finitely generated. -/
194	theorem FG.exists_span_finset_card_eq_spanFinrank {p : Submodule R M} (h : p.FG) :
195	    ∃ s : Finset M, s.card = p.spanFinrank ∧ span R s = p := by
196	  obtain ⟨s, ⟨hs₁, hs₂⟩⟩ := exists_span_set_encard_eq_spanFinrank h
197	  have s_f := Set.finite_of_encard_eq_coe hs₁
198	  refine ⟨s_f.toFinset, ⟨?_, by simpa using hs₂⟩⟩
199	  simpa [s_f.encard_eq_coe_toFinset_card, ENat.coe_inj] using hs₁
200	
201	lemma lift_spanRank_le_iff_exists_span_set_card_le (p : Submodule R M) {a : Cardinal.{max u v}} :
202	    Cardinal.lift.{v} p.spanRank ≤ a ↔ ∃ s : Set M, Cardinal.lift.{v} #s ≤ a ∧ span R s = p := by
203	  constructor
204	  · intro h
205	    obtain ⟨s, ⟨hs₁, hs₂⟩⟩ := exists_span_set_card_eq_spanRank p
206	    exact ⟨s, ⟨hs₁ ▸ h, hs₂⟩⟩
207	  · exact fun ⟨s, ⟨h₁, h₂⟩⟩ ↦ h₂.symm ▸ (Cardinal.lift_le.mpr (spanRank_span_le_card s)).trans h₁
208	
209	/-- For a finitely generated submodule, its spanRank is less than or equal to a cardinal `a`
210	  if and only if there is a generating subset with cardinality less than or equal to `a`. -/
211	lemma FG.spanRank_le_iff_exists_span_set_card_le (p : Submodule R M) {a : Cardinal} :
212	    p.spanRank ≤ a ↔ ∃ s : Set M, #s ≤ a ∧ span R s = p := by
213	  convert lift_spanRank_le_iff_exists_span_set_card_le p (a := a) <;> simp
214	
215	@[simp]
216	lemma spanRank_eq_zero_iff_eq_bot {I : Submodule R M} : I.spanRank = 0 ↔ I = ⊥ := by
217	  constructor
218	  · intro h
219	    obtain ⟨s, ⟨hs₁, hs₂⟩⟩ :=
220	      (FG.spanRank_le_iff_exists_span_set_card_le I (a := 0)).mp (by rw [h])
221	    simp only [nonpos_iff_eq_zero, mk_eq_zero_iff, Set.isEmpty_coe_sort] at hs₁
222	    simp_all
223	  · rintro rfl; rw [spanRank]
224	    exact Cardinal.iInf_eq_zero_iff.mpr (Or.inr ⟨⟨∅, by simp⟩, by simp⟩)
225	
226	@[simp]
227	lemma spanRank_bot : (⊥ : Ideal R).spanRank = 0 := Submodule.spanRank_eq_zero_iff_eq_bot.mpr rfl
228	
229	@[simp]
230	lemma spanFinrank_bot : (⊥ : Submodule R M).spanFinrank = 0 := by simp [spanFinrank]
231	
232	@[nontriviality]
233	lemma spanRank_subsingleton [Subsingleton R] (p : Submodule R M) : p.spanRank = 0 := by
234	  simp [nontriviality]
235	
236	@[nontriviality]
237	lemma spanFinrank_subsingleton [Subsingleton R] (p : Submodule R M) : p.spanFinrank = 0 := by
238	  have := Module.subsingleton R M
239	  simp [Submodule.eq_bot_of_subsingleton]
240	
241	/-- Generating elements for the submodule of minimum cardinality. -/
242	noncomputable def generators (p : Submodule R M) : Set M :=
243	  Classical.choose (exists_span_set_card_eq_spanRank p)
244	
245	lemma generators_card (p : Submodule R M) : #(generators p) = spanRank p :=
246	  (Classical.choose_spec (exists_span_set_card_eq_spanRank p)).1
247	
248	lemma FG.generators_ncard {p : Submodule R M} (h : p.FG) :
249	    (generators p).ncard = spanFinrank p := by
250	  rw [← Nat.cast_inj (R := Cardinal), ← fg_iff_spanRank_eq_spanFinrank.mpr h, Set.ncard, Set.encard,
251	     ENat.card, generators_card, toNat_toENat, ← spanFinrank]
252	  exact (fg_iff_spanRank_eq_spanFinrank.mpr h).symm
253	
254	lemma FG.finite_generators {p : Submodule R M} (hp : p.FG) :
255	    p.generators.Finite := by
256	  rw [← Cardinal.lt_aleph0_iff_set_finite, Submodule.generators_card]
257	  exact spanRank_finite_iff_fg.mpr hp
258	
259	/-- The span of the generators equals the submodule. -/
260	lemma span_generators (p : Submodule R M) : span R (generators p) = p :=
261	  (Classical.choose_spec (exists_span_set_card_eq_spanRank p)).2
262	
263	/-- The elements of the generators are in the submodule. -/
264	lemma FG.generators_mem (p : Submodule R M) : generators p ⊆ p := by
265	  nth_rw 2 [← span_generators p]
266	  exact subset_span (s := generators p)
267	
268	lemma spanRank_sup_le_sum_spanRank {p q : Submodule R M} :
269	    (p ⊔ q).spanRank ≤ p.spanRank + q.spanRank := by
270	  apply (FG.spanRank_le_iff_exists_span_set_card_le (p ⊔ q)).mpr
271	  obtain ⟨sp, ⟨hp₁, rfl⟩⟩ := exists_span_set_card_eq_spanRank p
272	  obtain ⟨sq, ⟨hq₁, rfl⟩⟩ := exists_span_set_card_eq_spanRank q
273	  exact ⟨sp ∪ sq, ⟨hp₁ ▸ hq₁ ▸ (Cardinal.mk_union_le sp sq), span_union sp sq⟩⟩
274	
275	lemma spanFinrank_eq_zero_iff_eq_bot {p : Submodule R M} (h : p.FG) :
276	    p.spanFinrank = 0 ↔ p = ⊥ := by
277	  refine ⟨fun heq ↦ ?_, fun h ↦ by simp [h]⟩
278	  rw [← Submodule.FG.generators_ncard h, Set.ncard_eq_zero h.finite_generators] at heq
279	  rw [← p.span_generators, heq, span_empty]
280	
281	lemma spanFinrank_singleton {m : M} (hm : m ≠ 0) : (span R {m}).spanFinrank = 1 := by
282	  apply le_antisymm ?_ ?_
283	  · exact le_trans (Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite (by simp)) (by simp)
284	  · by_contra!
285	    simp [Submodule.spanFinrank_eq_zero_iff_eq_bot (fg_span_singleton m), hm] at this
286	
287	end Defs
288	
289	end Submodule
290

300	  {L : Type v} [AddCommMonoid L] [Module S L]

24:* `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace`: The minimal number of
93:lemma spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace [IsNoetherianRing R] :

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Nailin Guan. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Nailin Guan
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.SpanRank
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Cotangent
10	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.LocalRing.Module
11	
12	/-!
13	# Span rank under operations
14	
15	In this file we show how operations on submodules interact with `Submodule.spanRank`.
16	
17	# Main Results
18	
19	* `Submodule.spanRank_baseChange_le`: Base change doesn't increase the span rank.
20	
21	* `TensorProduct.spanFinrank_top_eq_of_residueField`: For a finitely generated module over
22	  a local ring, the dimension of the base change to the residue field is equal to its span rank.
23	
24	* `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace`: The minimal number of
25	  generators of the unique maximal ideal is equal to the dimension of the cotangent space.
26	
27	-/
28	
29	@[expose] public section
30	
31	open IsLocalRing TensorProduct Submodule
32	
33	variable {R A : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing A] [Algebra R A]
34	  {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] (N : Submodule R M)
35	
36	lemma Submodule.spanRank_baseChange_le : (N.baseChange A).spanRank ≤ N.spanRank.lift := by
37	  obtain ⟨s, hs₁, hs₂⟩ := N.exists_span_set_card_eq_spanRank
38	  grw [← hs₁, ← hs₂, baseChange_span, spanRank_span_le_card]
39	  convert Cardinal.mk_image_le_lift (f := TensorProduct.mk R A M 1) (s := s)
40	  · exact (Cardinal.lift_id' _).symm
41	  · exact Cardinal.lift_umax.symm
42	
43	lemma Submodule.FG.spanFinrank_baseChange_le (fg : N.FG) :
44	    (N.baseChange A).spanFinrank ≤ N.spanFinrank := by
45	  grw [spanFinrank, spanRank_baseChange_le, Cardinal.toNat_lift, spanFinrank]
46	  simp [Cardinal.lift_lt_aleph0, spanRank_finite_iff_fg.mpr fg]
47	
48	lemma TensorProduct.spanRank_top_le : (⊤ : Submodule A (A ⊗[R] N)).spanRank ≤ N.spanRank.lift := by
49	  grw [← Submodule.baseChange_top, ← N.spanRank_top, spanRank_baseChange_le]
50	
51	lemma TensorProduct.spanFinrank_top_le_of_fg (fg : N.FG) :
52	    (⊤ : Submodule A (A ⊗[R] N)).spanFinrank ≤ N.spanFinrank := by
53	  grw [← Submodule.baseChange_top, ← N.spanFinrank_top, (N.fg_top.mpr fg).spanFinrank_baseChange_le]
54	
55	variable [IsLocalRing R]
56	local notation "𝓀" => ResidueField R
57	
58	lemma TensorProduct.spanFinrank_top_eq_of_residueField (fg : N.FG) :
59	    (⊤ : Submodule 𝓀 (𝓀 ⊗[R] N)).spanFinrank = N.spanFinrank := by
60	  let : Module.Finite R N := Module.Finite.iff_fg.mpr fg
61	  apply (TensorProduct.spanFinrank_top_le_of_fg N fg).antisymm
62	  obtain ⟨s, hs₁, hs₂⟩ := (⊤ : Submodule 𝓀 (𝓀 ⊗[R] N)).exists_span_set_card_eq_spanRank
63	  have hs₃ : s.Finite := Cardinal.mk_lt_aleph0_iff.mp (by simpa [hs₁] using Module.Finite.fg_top)
64	  let t := Function.surjInv (mk_surjective R N 𝓀 residue_surjective) '' s
65	  have ht₁ : mk R 𝓀 N 1 '' t = s := by rw [← Set.image_comp, Function.comp_surjInv, s.image_id]
66	  have ht₂ : span R t = ⊤ := by
67	    rwa [← restrictScalars_eq_top_iff R, restrictScalars_span _ _ (by exact residue_surjective),
68	      ← ht₁, ← map_span, map_tensorProduct_mk_eq_top] at hs₂
69	  grw [← N.spanFinrank_top, ← ht₂, spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite (hs₃.image _), spanFinrank,
70	    ← hs₁, Set.ncard_image_le hs₃]
71	  rfl
72	
73	namespace IsLocalRing
74	
75	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
76	lemma spanFinrank_eq_finrank_quotient (N : Submodule R M) (fg : N.FG) :
77	    N.spanFinrank =
78	      Module.finrank (R ⧸ maximalIdeal R) (N ⧸ (maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R N)) := by
79	  let : Module 𝓀 (N ⧸ maximalIdeal R • (⊤ : Submodule R N)) :=
80	    inferInstanceAs (Module (R ⧸ maximalIdeal R) _)
81	  let : IsScalarTower R 𝓀 (N ⧸ maximalIdeal R • (⊤ : Submodule R N)) :=
82	    inferInstanceAs (IsScalarTower R (R ⧸ maximalIdeal R) _)
83	  rw [← spanFinrank_top_eq_of_residueField N fg, ← Module.finrank_eq_spanFinrank_of_free]
84	  let e : 𝓀 ⊗[R] N ≃ₗ[𝓀] N ⧸ (maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R N) :=
85	    (quotTensorEquivQuotSMul N (maximalIdeal R)).extendScalarsOfSurjective residue_surjective
86	  exact e.finrank_eq
87	
88	lemma spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace_of_fg (fg : (maximalIdeal R).FG) :
89	    (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = Module.finrank (ResidueField R) (CotangentSpace R) :=
90	  spanFinrank_eq_finrank_quotient _ fg
91	
92	variable (R) in
93	lemma spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace [IsNoetherianRing R] :
94	    (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = Module.finrank (ResidueField R) (CotangentSpace R) :=
95	  spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace_of_fg (maximalIdeal R).fg_of_isNoetherianRing
96	
97	end IsLocalRing
98

  -- images of the remaining `spanFinrank - 1` generators form a minimal
  -- generating set of `𝔪 (R/(x))`. The `≤` direction is

input.new_string:
rw [← IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace R,
      ← IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace
          (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x})]
  -- Goal: (𝔪 (R/(x))).spanFinrank + 1 = (𝔪 R).spanFinrank
  -- iter-188 Lane G sub-lane G1: close via (≥) lift-and-cons + (≤) cotangent
  -- rank-nullity.
  refine le_antisymm ?_ ?_
  · -- (≤): (𝔪 (R/(x))).spanFinrank + 1 ≤ (𝔪 R).spanFinrank.
    -- **iter-188 progress note**: this is the Nakayama-extension direction.
    -- The substantive cotangent-space rank-nullity argument requires the
    -- κ-vs-κ' residue-field identification (κ = R/𝔪 vs κ' = (R/(x))/𝔪').
    -- The two are canonically isomorphic via `DoubleQuot.quotQuotEquivQuotSup`
    -- applied at I = (x), J = 𝔪 (gives (R/(x))/(𝔪/(x)) ≃+* R/((x)⊔𝔪) = R/𝔪 = κ).
    -- The κ-linear surjection mapCotangent : 𝔪.Cotangent → 𝔪'.Cotangent (via
    -- the iso) is surjective with 1-dim kernel κ-span{x̄}; rank-nullity then
    -- gives the equation. Bookkeeping for the residue-field switch is left
    -- as a typed sorry for iter-189+.
    sorry
  · -- (≥): (𝔪 R).spanFinrank ≤ (𝔪 (R/(x))).spanFinrank + 1.
    -- Lift-and-cons strategy: a min gen set T of 𝔪 (R/(x)) lifts to T_lift ⊆
    -- 𝔪 R via `Function.surjInv` of `Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective`; the union
    -- `T_lift ∪ {x}` generates 𝔪 R since 𝔪 R = (Ideal.span {x}) ⊔ (lift of 𝔪').
    set R' : Type u := R ⧸ Ideal.span {x} with hR'_def
    let mkx : R →+* R' := Ideal.Quotient.mk _
    let g : R' → R := Function.surjInv Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
    have hg : ∀ y, mkx (g y) = y := Function.surjInv_eq _
    -- Get min gen finset of 𝔪'.
    have h𝔪'_fg : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R').FG := Ideal.fg_of_isNoetherianRing _
    obtain ⟨T, hT_card, hT_span⟩ := h𝔪'_fg.exists_span_finset_card_eq_spanFinrank
    -- T : Finset R', T.card = spanFinrank 𝔪', span R' T = 𝔪'.
    let T_lift : Finset R := T.image g
    let U : Finset R := insert x T_lift
    -- Step A: U generates 𝔪 R via the comap identification.
    -- Comap mkx (𝔪 R') = 𝔪 R, since R is local and mkx is surjective.
    have hcomap_eq : Ideal.comap mkx (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R') =
        IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
      have hmax : (Ideal.comap mkx (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R')).IsMaximal :=
        Ideal.comap_isMaximal_of_surjective _ Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
      exact (IsLocalRing.isMaximal_iff R).mp hmax
    have hker_eq : RingHom.ker mkx = Ideal.span {x} := Ideal.mk_ker
    -- Step B: 𝔪 R = (Ideal.span T_lift) ⊔ (Ideal.span {x}).
    -- Compute: comap mkx (map mkx (Ideal.span T_lift)) = Ideal.span T_lift ⊔ ker mkx.
    have hmap_T_lift : Ideal.map mkx (Ideal.span (T_lift : Set R)) =
        IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R' := by
      rw [Ideal.map_span]
      -- mkx '' T_lift = T (as sets).
      have him : mkx '' (T_lift : Set R) = (T : Set R') := by
        ext y
        simp only [Set.mem_image, Finset.coe_image, T_lift]
        refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
        · rintro ⟨a, ⟨b, hbT, rfl⟩, rfl⟩
          rw [hg b]; exact hbT
        · intro hyT
          exact ⟨g y, ⟨y, hyT, rfl⟩, hg y⟩
      rw [him]; exact hT_span
    have h𝔪R_decomp : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R =
        Ideal.span (T_lift : Set R) ⊔ Ideal.span {x} := by
      calc IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R
          = Ideal.comap mkx (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R') := hcomap_eq.symm
        _ = Ideal.comap mkx (Ideal.map mkx (Ideal.span (T_lift : Set R))) := by
              rw [hmap_T_lift]
        _ = Ideal.span (T_lift : Set R) ⊔ RingHom.ker mkx := by
              rw [Ideal.comap_map_of_surjective' mkx Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective]
        _ = Ideal.span (T_lift : Set R) ⊔ Ideal.span {x} := by rw [hker_eq]
    -- Step C: span R U = Ideal.span T_lift ⊔ Ideal.span {x}.
    have hU_span_eq : Submodule.span R (↑U : Set R) =
        Ideal.span (T_lift : Set R) ⊔ Ideal.span {x} := by
      have : (↑U : Set R) = (↑T_lift : Set R) ∪ {x} := by
        simp only [U, Finset.coe_insert, Set.insert_eq, Set.union_comm]
      rw [this, Submodule.span_union]
      rfl
    -- Step D: spanFinrank 𝔪 R ≤ U.card ≤ T.card + 1.
    calc Submodule.spanFinrank (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
        ≤ (↑U : Set R).ncard := by
          rw [← h𝔪R_decomp, ← hU_span_eq]
          exact Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite U.finite_toSet
      _ = U.card := Set.ncard_coe_Finset _
      _ ≤ T_lift.card + 1 := by
          have := Finset.card_insert_le x T_lift
          simpa [U] using this
      _ ≤ T.card + 1 := by
          have : T_lift.card ≤ T.card := by simp [T_lift, Finset.card_image_le]
          omega
      _ = Submodule.spanFinrank (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R') + 1 := by
          rw [hT_card]

· -- (≤): (𝔪 (R/(x))).spanFinrank + 1 ≤ (𝔪 R).spanFinrank.
    -- iter-188 Lane G sub-lane G1 partial: cotangent-space rank-nullity
    -- approach. We reduce to the cotangent-space finrank inequality
    -- `finrank κ' (𝔪'.Cotangent) + 1 ≤ finrank κ (𝔪.Cotangent)` via the
    -- spanFinrank-cotangent bridge, then prove it using:
    -- (a) Helper 1.5: x̄ ≠ 0 in 𝔪.Cotangent (axiom-clean, landed iter-187).
    -- (b) R-linear surjection mapCotangent : 𝔪.Cotangent → 𝔪'.Cotangent with
    --     kernel = R-span{x̄} = κ-span{x̄} (1-dim κ-subspace).
    -- (c) Rank-nullity over κ on 𝔪.Cotangent quotient by κ-span{x̄}.
    -- (d) κ ≃+* κ' residue-field switch via `DoubleQuot.quotQuotEquivQuotSup`
    --     applied at I = (x), J = 𝔪 (giving (R/(x))/(𝔪/(x)) ≃+* R/((x)⊔𝔪) = R/𝔪 = κ).
    -- (e) finrank-preservation under the κ ≃+* κ' iso (via
    --     `Algebra.finrank_eq_of_equiv_equiv` or `Module.compHom`).
    --
    -- The structural setup below carries out (a)-(c); the κ → κ' iso (d)
    -- + finrank transfer (e) is the residual narrow named sorry.
    rw [show Submodule.spanFinrank (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}))
            = Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}))
                (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x})) from
        IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace _,
        show Submodule.spanFinrank (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
            = Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)
                (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace R) from
        IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace _]
    -- Goal: finrank κ' (CotangentSpace R') + 1 ≤ finrank κ (CotangentSpace R)
    -- Helper 1.5 input: x̄ ≠ 0 in 𝔪.Cotangent.
    have hx_bar_ne : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).toCotangent
        ⟨x, hx⟩ ≠ 0 := toCotangent_ne_zero_of_not_mem_sq x hx hxnotsq
    -- iter-189+ residual: the κ → κ' residue-field iso + finrank transfer
    -- bookkeeping. The substantive cotangent-space rank-nullity argument
    -- is structurally complete in the comment above; closing this typed
    -- sorry requires ~50-80 LOC of κ-module/finrank bookkeeping.
    sorry
input.new_string:
· -- (≤): (𝔪 (R/(x))).spanFinrank + 1 ≤ (𝔪 R).spanFinrank.
    -- iter-188 Lane G sub-lane G1: Steinitz-exchange strategy.
    -- Take a min gen finset V of 𝔪 R of size n. Express x = ∑ cᵥ v in
    -- R-linear combination of V. Since x ∉ 𝔪², some coefficient cᵥ₀ ∉ 𝔪,
    -- hence is a unit (R local). Then v₀ ∈ Ideal.span ({x} ∪ V \ {v₀}),
    -- so 𝔪 R = Ideal.span ({x} ∪ V.erase v₀). Applying mkx (which kills x),
    -- 𝔪 R' = Ideal.map mkx 𝔪 R = Ideal.span (mkx '' (V.erase v₀)), a set
    -- of cardinality ≤ n - 1.
    classical
    set R' : Type u := R ⧸ Ideal.span {x} with hR'_def
    let mkx : R →+* R' := Ideal.Quotient.mk _
    -- Get min gen finset V of 𝔪 R.
    have h𝔪_fg : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R).FG := Ideal.fg_of_isNoetherianRing _
    obtain ⟨V, hV_card, hV_span⟩ :=
      Submodule.FG.exists_span_finset_card_eq_spanFinrank h𝔪_fg
    -- Step 1: spanFinrank 𝔪 R ≥ 1 (since x ∉ 𝔪² implies x ≠ 0, hence 𝔪 ≠ ⊥).
    have h_n_ge_1 : 1 ≤ Submodule.spanFinrank (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) := by
      rw [← hV_card]
      by_contra h
      push_neg at h
      have hV_empty : V.card = 0 := Nat.lt_one_iff.mp h
      have hV_eq : V = ∅ := Finset.card_eq_zero.mp hV_empty
      have h𝔪_bot : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R = ⊥ := by
        rw [← hV_span, hV_eq, Finset.coe_empty, Submodule.span_empty]
      apply hxnotsq
      rw [h𝔪_bot]; simp
    -- Step 2: x ∈ Submodule.span R V, extract coefficients via mem_span_finset.
    have hx_mem : x ∈ Submodule.span R (V : Set R) := hV_span ▸ hx
    obtain ⟨c, _hc_supp, hc_sum⟩ := Submodule.mem_span_finset.mp hx_mem
    -- Step 3: ∃ v₀ ∈ V with c v₀ ∉ 𝔪 R (i.e., c v₀ is a unit).
    -- If all c v ∈ 𝔪, then x = Σ c v • v ∈ 𝔪 · 𝔪 = 𝔪². Contradicts hxnotsq.
    have hexists_unit : ∃ v₀ ∈ V, c v₀ ∉ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
      by_contra h
      push_neg at h
      apply hxnotsq
      rw [pow_two, ← hc_sum]
      refine Submodule.sum_mem _ ?_
      intro v hvV
      have hcv_mem : c v ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := h v hvV
      have hv_mem : v ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
        rw [← hV_span]; exact Submodule.subset_span (by exact_mod_cast hvV)
      -- c v • v = c v * v ∈ 𝔪 * 𝔪 = 𝔪 • 𝔪.
      have : c v • v ∈ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R : Submodule R R) *
          IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
        rw [smul_eq_mul]
        exact Ideal.mul_mem_mul hcv_mem hv_mem
      simpa [Ideal.smul_eq_mul] using this
    obtain ⟨v₀, hv₀_V, hv₀_notmem⟩ := hexists_unit
    have hv₀_unit : IsUnit (c v₀) := IsLocalRing.notMem_maximalIdeal.mp hv₀_notmem
    obtain ⟨u, hu⟩ := hv₀_unit
    -- u : Rˣ with ↑u = c v₀.
    -- Step 4: 𝔪 R = Ideal.span (insert x (V.erase v₀)).
    -- We show: v₀ ∈ Ideal.span (insert x (V.erase v₀)).
    have hv₀_in_new : v₀ ∈ Submodule.span R (insert x (V.erase v₀ : Set R)) := by
      -- v₀ = u⁻¹ · (x - Σ_{v ∈ V.erase v₀} c v • v)
      -- because c v₀ * v₀ = x - Σ_{v ∈ V.erase v₀} c v • v (from hc_sum).
      have hsum_split : c v₀ * v₀ + ∑ v ∈ V.erase v₀, c v • v = x := by
        rw [← Finset.sum_erase_add _ _ hv₀_V] at hc_sum
        simp [smul_eq_mul] at hc_sum
        linarith_or_polyrith
      sorry  -- TODO: extract v₀ from the equation
    -- Step 5: span (insert x (V.erase v₀)) = span V.
    -- One direction: V ⊆ span (insert x (V.erase v₀)) — for v = v₀, use hv₀_in_new;
    -- for v ≠ v₀, v ∈ V.erase v₀ ⊆ insert x (V.erase v₀).
    -- Other direction: insert x (V.erase v₀) ⊆ 𝔪 = span V — x ∈ 𝔪 = span V,
    -- V.erase v₀ ⊆ V ⊆ span V.
    sorry

      -- v₀ = u⁻¹ * (x - Σ_{v ∈ V.erase v₀} c v • v)
      -- because c v₀ * v₀ = x - Σ_{v ∈ V.erase v₀} c v • v (from hc_sum).
      have hsum_split : c v₀ * v₀ + ∑ v ∈ V.erase v₀, c v * v = x := by
        rw [← Finset.sum_erase_add _ _ hv₀_V] at hc_sum
        simp only [smul_eq_mul] at hc_sum
        linarith
      -- So v₀ = u⁻¹ * x + Σ_{v ∈ V.erase v₀} (-(u⁻¹ * c v)) * v.
      have hu_inv : (u⁻¹ : R) * c v₀ = 1 := by
        rw [← hu]; exact Units.inv_mul u
      have hv₀_eq : v₀ = (↑u⁻¹ : R) * x +
          ∑ v ∈ V.erase v₀, (-(↑u⁻¹ : R) * c v) * v := by
        have : (↑u⁻¹ : R) * (c v₀ * v₀ + ∑ v ∈ V.erase v₀, c v * v) =
              (↑u⁻¹ : R) * x := by rw [hsum_split]
        rw [mul_add, ← mul_assoc, hu_inv, one_mul, Finset.mul_sum] at this
        linarith
      rw [hv₀_eq]
      refine Submodule.add_mem _ ?_ (Submodule.sum_mem _ ?_)
      · exact Submodule.smul_mem _ _
          (Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_insert _ _))
      · intro v hvErase
        refine Submodule.smul_mem _ _ ?_
        exact Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_insert_of_mem _ (by exact_mod_cast hvErase))
    -- Step 5: span R (insert x (V.erase v₀)) = 𝔪 R.
    have h𝔪R_new : Submodule.span R (insert x (V.erase v₀ : Set R)) =
        IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
      apply le_antisymm
      · -- insert x (V.erase v₀) ⊆ 𝔪 R: x ∈ 𝔪, V.erase v₀ ⊆ V ⊆ 𝔪.
        rw [Submodule.span_le]
        rintro y hy
        rcases hy with rfl | hy
        · exact hx
        · have hy_V : y ∈ V := Finset.mem_of_mem_erase (by exact_mod_cast hy)
          rw [← hV_span]
          exact Submodule.subset_span (by exact_mod_cast hy_V)
      · -- 𝔪 R = span V ⊆ span (insert x (V.erase v₀)).
        rw [← hV_span, Submodule.span_le]
        intro v hv
        by_cases hv_eq : v = v₀
        · rw [hv_eq]; exact hv₀_in_new
        · refine Submodule.subset_span ?_
          right
          exact_mod_cast Finset.mem_erase.mpr ⟨hv_eq, by exact_mod_cast hv⟩
    -- Step 6: 𝔪 R' = Ideal.span (mkx '' (V.erase v₀)).
    -- 𝔪 R' = Ideal.map mkx 𝔪 R (image equals 𝔪'); applying Ideal.map_span
    -- and mkx x = 0 collapses to the desired set.
    have h_mkx_x : mkx x = 0 := by
      change (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {x})) x = 0
      rw [Ideal.Quotient.eq_zero_iff_mem]
      exact Submodule.mem_span_singleton_self x
    have hcomap_eq : Ideal.comap mkx (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R') =
        IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
      have hmax : (Ideal.comap mkx (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R')).IsMaximal :=
        Ideal.comap_isMaximal_of_surjective _ Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
      exact (IsLocalRing.isMaximal_iff R).mp hmax
    have h𝔪R'_eq : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R' =
        Ideal.map mkx (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) := by
      conv_lhs => rw [← hcomap_eq]
      exact (Ideal.map_comap_of_surjective _ Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective _).symm
    -- 𝔪 R' = Ideal.map mkx (span (insert x (V.erase v₀)))
    --      = Ideal.span (mkx '' insert x (V.erase v₀))
    --      = Ideal.span (insert 0 (mkx '' V.erase v₀))
    --      = Ideal.span (mkx '' V.erase v₀).
    have h𝔪R'_span_image : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R' =
        Ideal.span (mkx '' (V.erase v₀ : Set R)) := by
      rw [h𝔪R'_eq, ← h𝔪R_new, Ideal.map_span]
      congr 1
      ext y
      simp only [Set.mem_image, Set.mem_insert_iff]
      refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
      · rintro ⟨a, (rfl | haErase), rfl⟩
        · -- mkx a where a = x, so y = mkx x = 0. We want y ∈ mkx '' V.erase v₀.
          -- Since 0 ∈ Ideal.span (mkx '' V.erase v₀), this should work by
          -- pulling 0 in. But here we want y to be in the SET. Actually we want
          -- the spans equal. So this `congr 1` may not work — let me re-do.
          sorry
        · exact ⟨a, haErase, rfl⟩
      · rintro ⟨a, haErase, rfl⟩
        exact ⟨a, Or.inr haErase, rfl⟩
    -- Step 7: spanFinrank 𝔪 R' ≤ (mkx '' (V.erase v₀)).ncard ≤ V.erase v₀.card = n - 1.
    have h_bound : Submodule.spanFinrank (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R') ≤
        V.card - 1 := by
      calc Submodule.spanFinrank (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R')
          ≤ (mkx '' (V.erase v₀ : Set R)).ncard := by
            rw [h𝔪R'_span_image]
            exact Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite
              ((V.erase v₀).finite_toSet.image mkx)
        _ ≤ ((V.erase v₀ : Set R).ncard) :=
            Set.ncard_image_le ((V.erase v₀).finite_toSet)
        _ = (V.erase v₀).card := by simp
        _ = V.card - 1 := Finset.card_erase_of_mem hv₀_V
    omega

## finrank_cotangentSpace_quot_span_singleton_succ (line 984)

**Target (iter-188 Lane G sub-lane G1)**: close the spanFinrank dim-drop
equation `(𝔪 (R/(x))).spanFinrank + 1 = (𝔪 R).spanFinrank`.

  strategy. After the existing rewrites via

  into the two inequalities.
- **Result**: PARTIAL — (≥) direction closed kernel-clean (~50 LOC).

  from full closure).

### (≥) direction (CLOSED, axiom-clean, ~50 LOC)

**Strategy**: lift a min gen finset `T` of `𝔪 (R/(x))` to `T_lift ⊆ 𝔪 R`
via `Function.surjInv (Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective)`; the union
`T_lift ∪ {x}` generates `𝔪 R` since `𝔪 R = (Ideal.span {x}) ⊔ (lift of 𝔪')`
via `Ideal.comap_map_of_surjective'`.

**Key Mathlib lemmas used**:
- `Submodule.FG.exists_span_finset_card_eq_spanFinrank` (extract min gen
  finset matching spanFinrank).
- `Ideal.comap_map_of_surjective'` (`comap f (map f I) = I ⊔ ker f`).
- `IsLocalRing.isMaximal_iff` (R local: `IsMaximal I ↔ I = maximalIdeal R`).
- `Ideal.map_span` (image of span equals span of image).
- `Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite` (cardinality bound).

### (≤) direction (PARTIAL, Steps 1-3 axiom-clean ~25 LOC, Steps 4-7 typed sorry)

**Strategy**: Steinitz-exchange. Take min gen finset V of 𝔪 R of size n.
Express x as R-linear combo of V; since `x ∉ 𝔪²`, some coefficient `c v₀ ∉ 𝔪`,
hence is a unit (R local). Then `v₀ ∈ Ideal.span (insert x (V.erase v₀))`,
so `𝔪 R = Ideal.span (insert x (V.erase v₀))`. Applying mkx (which kills x),
`𝔪 R' = Ideal.span (mkx '' (V.erase v₀))`, a set of cardinality ≤ n - 1.

**Closed steps (Steps 1-3, axiom-clean)**:
1. **Step 1**: `1 ≤ (𝔪 R).spanFinrank` (via `x ∉ 𝔪² ⇒ x ≠ 0 ⇒ 𝔪 ≠ ⊥`).
2. **Step 2**: extract `c : R → R` with `∑ a ∈ V, c a • a = x` via

**Residual narrow typed sorry (Steps 4-7, ~30-50 LOC, iter-189+)**:
4. **Step 4**: `v₀ ∈ Submodule.span R (insert x (V.erase v₀))`. Extract via

5. **Step 5**: `Submodule.span R (insert x (V.erase v₀)) = 𝔪 R`. Both
   inclusions are straightforward given Step 4 and `hV_span`.
6. **Step 6**: `𝔪 R' = Ideal.span (mkx '' (V.erase v₀))`. Via
   `Ideal.map_comap_of_surjective` + `Ideal.map_span` + `mkx x = 0`
   (collapsing `insert (mkx x) (mkx '' V.erase v₀) = insert 0 ...`).
7. **Step 7**: `spanFinrank 𝔪 R' ≤ |mkx '' V.erase v₀| ≤ V.card - 1`.
   Combined with `h_n_ge_1`, conclude `spanFinrank 𝔪' + 1 ≤ spanFinrank 𝔪 R`.

have h_mkx_x : (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {x}) : R →+* R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}) x = 0 :=

have hcomap_eq : Ideal.comap (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {x}))

  rw [← hcomap_eq]

    _ = V.card - 1 := Finset.card_erase_of_mem hv₀_V
rw [← hV_card]

    have hv₀_unit : IsUnit (c v₀) := IsLocalRing.notMem_maximalIdeal.mp hv₀_notmem
    -- Steps 4-7 (residual narrow named sorry, iter-189+):
    --   Step 4: v₀ ∈ Ideal.span (insert x (V.erase v₀)) (extract via hv₀_unit).
    --   Step 5: Submodule.span R (insert x (V.erase v₀)) = 𝔪 R.
    --   Step 6: 𝔪 R' = Ideal.span (mkx '' (V.erase v₀)) (apply mkx; mkx x = 0).
    --   Step 7: spanFinrank 𝔪 R' ≤ |V.erase v₀| = n - 1, conclude via h_n_ge_1.

input.new_string:
obtain ⟨v₀, hv₀_V, hv₀_notmem⟩ := hexists_unit
    have hv₀_unit : IsUnit (c v₀) := IsLocalRing.notMem_maximalIdeal.mp hv₀_notmem
    obtain ⟨u, hu⟩ := hv₀_unit
    -- Step 4: v₀ = ↑u⁻¹ * x - Σ_{v ∈ V.erase v₀} ↑u⁻¹ * c v * v.
    have hsum_split : c v₀ * v₀ + ∑ v ∈ V.erase v₀, c v * v = x := by
      rw [← Finset.sum_erase_add _ _ hv₀_V] at hc_sum
      simp only [smul_eq_mul] at hc_sum
      linear_combination hc_sum
    have hu_inv : (↑u⁻¹ : R) * c v₀ = 1 := by rw [← hu]; exact Units.inv_mul u
    have h_sum_eq : (↑u⁻¹ : R) * ∑ v ∈ V.erase v₀, c v * v =
        ∑ v ∈ V.erase v₀, (↑u⁻¹ : R) * c v * v := by
      rw [Finset.mul_sum]; apply Finset.sum_congr rfl; intros; ring
    have hv₀_eq : v₀ = (↑u⁻¹ : R) * x -
        ∑ v ∈ V.erase v₀, (↑u⁻¹ : R) * c v * v := by
      have h1 : (↑u⁻¹ : R) * (c v₀ * v₀ + ∑ v ∈ V.erase v₀, c v * v) =
          (↑u⁻¹ : R) * x := by rw [hsum_split]
      rw [mul_add, ← mul_assoc, hu_inv, one_mul, h_sum_eq] at h1
      linear_combination h1
    have hv₀_in_new : v₀ ∈ Submodule.span R (insert x (V.erase v₀ : Set R)) := by
      conv_lhs => rw [hv₀_eq]
      refine Submodule.sub_mem _ ?_ (Submodule.sum_mem _ ?_)
      · exact Submodule.smul_mem _ _
          (Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_insert _ _))
      · intro v hvErase
        exact Submodule.smul_mem _ _
          (Submodule.subset_span (Set.mem_insert_of_mem _
            (by exact_mod_cast hvErase)))
    -- Step 5: Submodule.span R (insert x (V.erase v₀)) = 𝔪 R.
    have h𝔪R_new : Submodule.span R (insert x (V.erase v₀ : Set R)) =
        IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
      apply le_antisymm
      · rw [Submodule.span_le]
        rintro y hy
        rcases hy with rfl | hy
        · exact hx
        · have hy_V : y ∈ V := Finset.mem_of_mem_erase (by exact_mod_cast hy)
          rw [← hV_span]
          exact Submodule.subset_span (by exact_mod_cast hy_V)
      · rw [← hV_span, Submodule.span_le]
        intro v hv
        by_cases hv_eq : v = v₀
        · rw [hv_eq]; exact hv₀_in_new
        · refine Submodule.subset_span ?_
          right
          exact_mod_cast Finset.mem_erase.mpr ⟨hv_eq, by exact_mod_cast hv⟩
    -- Step 6: 𝔪 R' = Ideal.span (mkx '' (V.erase v₀)).
    -- First, 𝔪 R' = Ideal.map mkx 𝔪 R, and mkx x = 0.
    set mkx : R →+* (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}) := Ideal.Quotient.mk _ with hmkx_def
    have h_mkx_x : mkx x = 0 :=
      Ideal.Quotient.eq_zero_iff_mem.mpr (Submodule.mem_span_singleton_self x)
    have hcomap_eq : Ideal.comap mkx
        (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x})) =
        IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
      have hmax : (Ideal.comap mkx
          (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}))).IsMaximal :=
        Ideal.comap_isMaximal_of_surjective _ Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
      exact (IsLocalRing.isMaximal_iff R).mp hmax
    have h𝔪R'_eq_map : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}) =
        Ideal.map mkx (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) := by
      conv_lhs => rw [← hcomap_eq]
      exact (Ideal.map_comap_of_surjective _ Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective _).symm
    -- Now 𝔪 R' = Ideal.map mkx (span (insert x (V.erase v₀)))
    --         = Ideal.span (mkx '' (insert x (V.erase v₀)))
    --         = Ideal.span (insert 0 (mkx '' (V.erase v₀)))
    --         ≤ Submodule.span R' (mkx '' (V.erase v₀)).
    -- For the spanFinrank bound it suffices to show the inequality
    -- spanFinrank 𝔪 R' ≤ |V.erase v₀|.
    have h_bound : Submodule.spanFinrank
        (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x})) ≤ V.card - 1 := by
      have h𝔪R'_span : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}) =
          Ideal.span (mkx '' (V.erase v₀ : Set R)) := by
        rw [h𝔪R'_eq_map, ← h𝔪R_new, Ideal.map_span,
            Set.image_insert_eq, h_mkx_x]
        -- Ideal.span (insert 0 (mkx '' V.erase v₀)) = Ideal.span (mkx '' V.erase v₀)
        rw [Set.insert_eq, Submodule.span_union]
        simp [Submodule.span_singleton_eq_bot.mpr rfl, bot_sup_eq]
      calc Submodule.spanFinrank
            (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}))
          = Submodule.spanFinrank
              (Ideal.span (mkx '' (V.erase v₀ : Set R))) := by rw [h𝔪R'_span]
        _ = Submodule.spanFinrank
              (Submodule.span _ (mkx '' (V.erase v₀ : Set R))) := rfl
        _ ≤ (mkx '' (V.erase v₀ : Set R)).ncard :=
            Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite
              ((V.erase v₀).finite_toSet.image _)
        _ ≤ (V.erase v₀ : Set R).ncard := Set.ncard_image_le _
        _ = (V.erase v₀).card := by simp
        _ = V.card - 1 := Finset.card_erase_of_mem hv₀_V
    -- Step 7: conclude using h_n_ge_1.
    omega

have hv₀_in_new : v₀ ∈ Submodule.span R (insert x (V.erase v₀ : Set R)) := by
      -- Prove the membership via the explicit linear combination, then
      -- rewrite v₀ on the LHS only via hv₀_eq.symm ▸.
      have key : (↑u⁻¹ : R) * x - ∑ v ∈ V.erase v₀, (↑u⁻¹ : R) * c v * v ∈
          Submodule.span R (insert x (V.erase v₀ : Set R)) := by
        apply Submodule.sub_mem

          intro v hvErase
          exact Submodule.smul_mem _ _

      exact hv₀_eq.symm ▸ key

# AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean

## finrank_cotangentSpace_quot_span_singleton_succ (line 984)

**Target (iter-188 Lane G sub-lane G1)**: close the spanFinrank dim-drop
equation `(𝔪 (R/(x))).spanFinrank + 1 = (𝔪 R).spanFinrank`.

### Attempt 1
- **Approach**: Split into (≥) lift-and-cons + (≤) Steinitz-exchange. After
  the existing rewrites via
  `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace`, reduce
  to spanFinrank equation and `le_antisymm` into two inequalities.
- **Result**: **RESOLVED axiom-clean**. Both directions closed kernel-only
  (`propext`, `Classical.choice`, `Quot.sound`; verified via `lean_verify`).
  Net file sorry count: **3 → 2** (−1).

### (≥) direction (CLOSED kernel-only, ~50 LOC)

**Strategy**: lift a min gen finset `T` of `𝔪 (R/(x))` to `T_lift ⊆ 𝔪 R`
via `Function.surjInv (Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective)`; the union
`T_lift ∪ {x}` generates `𝔪 R` since `𝔪 R = (Ideal.span {x}) ⊔ (lift of 𝔪')`
via `Ideal.comap_map_of_surjective'`.

**Key Mathlib lemmas used**:
- `Submodule.FG.exists_span_finset_card_eq_spanFinrank` (requires explicit
  namespace — dot notation fails because `Submodule.FG` is a definition).
- `Ideal.comap_map_of_surjective'` (`comap f (map f I) = I ⊔ ker f`).
- `IsLocalRing.isMaximal_iff` (R local: `IsMaximal I ↔ I = maximalIdeal R`).
- `Ideal.map_span`, `Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite`.

### (≤) direction (CLOSED kernel-only, ~95 LOC)

**Strategy**: Steinitz-exchange. Take min gen finset V of 𝔪 R of size n.
Express x as R-linear combo `x = ∑ c v • v` via `Submodule.mem_span_finset`.
Since `x ∉ 𝔪²`, some coefficient `c v₀ ∉ 𝔪` (Nakayama-style contradiction
via `Ideal.mul_mem_mul`), hence is a unit (`IsLocalRing.notMem_maximalIdeal`).
Then `v₀ ∈ Ideal.span (insert x (V.erase v₀))` via explicit linear extraction
using the unit's inverse:
`v₀ = (↑u⁻¹) * x - ∑_{v ∈ V.erase v₀} (↑u⁻¹) * c v * v`.
Hence `𝔪 R = Ideal.span (insert x (V.erase v₀))`. Applying mkx (which kills x),
`𝔪 R' = Ideal.span (mkx '' (V.erase v₀))`, a set of cardinality ≤ n - 1.

**Steps**:
1. **Step 1**: `1 ≤ (𝔪 R).spanFinrank` via `x ∉ 𝔪² ⇒ x ≠ 0 ⇒ 𝔪 ≠ ⊥`.
2. **Step 2**: extract coefficients via `Submodule.mem_span_finset`.
3. **Step 3 (axiom-clean Nakayama)**: `∃ v₀ ∈ V` with `c v₀ ∉ 𝔪` — via
   `Ideal.mul_mem_mul` showing all-c-in-𝔪 ⇒ x ∈ 𝔪².
4. **Step 4**: `v₀ ∈ Submodule.span R (insert x (V.erase v₀))` — via
   explicit `hv₀_eq` extraction (uses `linear_combination` + `rwa [← hv₀_eq]`
   to substitute only the LHS without touching V.erase v₀).
5. **Step 5**: `Submodule.span R (insert x (V.erase v₀)) = 𝔪 R` (≤ via
   `Submodule.span_le`; ≥ via case split on `v = v₀` or `v ∈ V.erase v₀`).
6. **Step 6**: `𝔪 R' = Ideal.span (mkx '' (V.erase v₀))` via
   `Ideal.map_comap_of_surjective` + `Ideal.map_span` + `Set.image_insert_eq`
   + `mkx x = 0` (collapsing `insert 0 (mkx '' V.erase v₀)`).
7. **Step 7**: `spanFinrank 𝔪 R' ≤ |V.erase v₀| = V.card - 1` via the
   chain `Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite + Set.ncard_image_le
   + Set.ncard_coe_finset + Finset.card_erase_of_mem`. Conclude via `omega`.

**Key Mathlib lemmas (all present at b80f227)**:
- `Submodule.mem_span_finset` (linear combination extraction).
- `Ideal.mul_mem_mul` (𝔪 * 𝔪 = 𝔪²).
- `IsLocalRing.notMem_maximalIdeal` (`x ∉ 𝔪 ↔ IsUnit x`).
- `linear_combination` (algebraic manipulation tactic).
- `Ideal.map_comap_of_surjective`, `Ideal.map_span`, `Set.image_insert_eq`.
- `Ideal.Quotient.eq_zero_iff_mem` (`mkx x = 0` from `x ∈ Ideal.span {x}`).
- `Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite`, `Set.ncard_image_le`,
  `Set.ncard_coe_finset` (cardinality bounds).
- `Finset.card_erase_of_mem` (count arithmetic).

### Technical lessons (for future provers)

1. **`rw` rewrites all occurrences**: When `v₀` appears both as a standalone
   term and inside `V.erase v₀`, `rw [hv₀_eq]` (where `hv₀_eq : v₀ = expr`)
   rewrites BOTH occurrences, breaking the second. Solution: prove the
   key membership with the explicit `expr` form, then use `rwa [← hv₀_eq] at`
   to substitute only the unique `expr` occurrence backwards. `nth_rewrite 1`
   and `conv_lhs` are not sufficient here.
2. **`Submodule.FG` dot-notation**: fails because `Submodule.FG` is a definition,
   not a structure. Use `Submodule.FG.exists_span_finset_card_eq_spanFinrank h`
   explicitly.
3. **`Set.ncard_coe_finset`** (lowercase `f`), not `_coe_Finset`.
4. **`Ideal.fg_of_isNoetherianRing`** (not `Submodule.fg_...`).
5. **`linear_combination` is essential** for ring-arithmetic equations
   involving sums where `ring` alone fails.

### Status summary

- Sorry count in file: 3 → 2 (−1).
- Net axiom count: 0 (no new project axioms introduced).
- `finrank_cotangentSpace_quot_span_singleton_succ` verified axiom-clean
  (kernel-only: `propext`, `Classical.choice`, `Quot.sound`).
- Lane G sub-lane G1: **CLOSED** (was the iter-188 HARD BAR target).
- Lane G sub-lane G2 (joint induction Stacks 00NQ, ~200 LOC): unblocked,
  proceed iter-189+.

### Blueprint marker recommendation for review agent

- `finrank_cotangentSpace_quot_span_singleton_succ` is a `private` helper
  with no `\lean{...}` pin in the blueprint chapter — no marker change needed.
- Downstream consumer `exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal` (which feeds
  `CohenMacaulay.of_regular`) remains gated on the unrelated sub-lane G2
  Stacks 00NQ substrate gap (`exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ`).
- No blueprint `\leanok` impact iter-188; the existing `\leanok` on
  `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` will stay until G2 closes (the dependency is
  already accounted-for in the blueprint chapter prose).

### iter-189+ next-step recipe

1. **Sub-lane G2 (joint induction Stacks 00NQ)**: now unblocked. The pending
   typed sorry `exists_isSMulRegular_quotient_isRegularLocal_succ` (L1286)
   consolidates Stacks 00NQ (IsRegularLocalRing ⟹ IsDomain, ~300 LOC) +
   Stacks 00NU (Krull's principal ideal theorem for regular quotient,
   ~200 LOC). The cotangent dim-drop helper closed iter-188 is the bridge
   between G1 and G2.
2. **Closing `of_regular` axiom-clean** then needs G2 closure + the existing
   structural scaffold in `exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal` (already
   axiom-clean modulo G2 substrate).

content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. **Requires `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in env** — check via `env | grep -E "OPENAI|GEMINI|OPENROUTER"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | — | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/` (`prover-autoformalize.md` / `prover-prover.md` / `prover-polish.md`).
127	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
128	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
129	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
130	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
131	
132	### Subagents
133	
134	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
135	
136	### Review Agent
137	
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
139	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
140	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
141	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
142	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	These run automatically between phases:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
150	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
151	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
152

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
