6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Topology.Connected.Basic
9	public import Mathlib.Topology.Connected.Clopen
10	
11	/-!
12	# Locally connected topological spaces
13	
14	A topological space is **locally connected** if each neighborhood filter admits a basis
15	of connected *open* sets. Local connectivity is equivalent to each point having a basis
16	of connected (not necessarily open) sets --- but in a non-trivial way, so we choose this definition
17	and prove the equivalence later in `locallyConnectedSpace_iff_connected_basis`.
18	-/
19	
20	@[expose] public section
21	
22	open Set Topology
23	
24	universe u v
25	
26	variable {α : Type u} {β : Type v} {ι : Type*} {X : ι → Type*} [TopologicalSpace α]
27	  {s t u v : Set α}
28	
29	section LocallyConnectedSpace
30	
31	/-- A topological space is **locally connected** if each neighborhood filter admits a basis
32	of connected *open* sets. Note that it is equivalent to each point having a basis of connected
33	(not necessarily open) sets but in a non-trivial way, so we choose this definition and prove the
34	equivalence later in `locallyConnectedSpace_iff_connected_basis`. -/
35	class LocallyConnectedSpace (α : Type*) [TopologicalSpace α] : Prop where
36	  /-- Open connected neighborhoods form a basis of the neighborhoods filter. -/
37	  open_connected_basis : ∀ x, (𝓝 x).HasBasis (fun s : Set α => IsOpen s ∧ x ∈ s ∧ IsConnected s) id
38	
39	theorem locallyConnectedSpace_iff_hasBasis_isOpen_isConnected :
40	    LocallyConnectedSpace α ↔
41	      ∀ x, (𝓝 x).HasBasis (fun s : Set α => IsOpen s ∧ x ∈ s ∧ IsConnected s) id :=
42	  ⟨@LocallyConnectedSpace.open_connected_basis _ _, LocallyConnectedSpace.mk⟩
43	
44	theorem locallyConnectedSpace_iff_subsets_isOpen_isConnected :
45	    LocallyConnectedSpace α ↔
46	      ∀ x, ∀ U ∈ 𝓝 x, ∃ V : Set α, V ⊆ U ∧ IsOpen V ∧ x ∈ V ∧ IsConnected V := by
47	  simp_rw [locallyConnectedSpace_iff_hasBasis_isOpen_isConnected]
48	  refine forall_congr' fun _ => ?_
49	  constructor
50	  · intro h U hU
51	    rcases h.mem_iff.mp hU with ⟨V, hV, hVU⟩
52	    exact ⟨V, hVU, hV⟩
53	  · exact fun h => ⟨fun U => ⟨fun hU =>
54	      let ⟨V, hVU, hV⟩ := h U hU
55	      ⟨V, hV, hVU⟩, fun ⟨V, ⟨hV, hxV, _⟩, hVU⟩ => mem_nhds_iff.mpr ⟨V, hVU, hV, hxV⟩⟩⟩
56	
57	/-- A space with discrete topology is a locally connected space. -/
58	instance (priority := 100) DiscreteTopology.toLocallyConnectedSpace (α) [TopologicalSpace α]
59	    [DiscreteTopology α] : LocallyConnectedSpace α :=
60	  locallyConnectedSpace_iff_subsets_isOpen_isConnected.2 fun x _U hU =>
61	    ⟨{x}, singleton_subset_iff.2 <| mem_of_mem_nhds hU, isOpen_discrete _, rfl,
62	      isConnected_singleton⟩
63	
64	theorem connectedComponentIn_mem_nhds [LocallyConnectedSpace α] {F : Set α} {x : α} (h : F ∈ 𝓝 x) :
65	    connectedComponentIn F x ∈ 𝓝 x := by
66	  rw [(LocallyConnectedSpace.open_connected_basis x).mem_iff] at h
67	  rcases h with ⟨s, ⟨h1s, hxs, h2s⟩, hsF⟩
68	  exact mem_nhds_iff.mpr ⟨s, h2s.isPreconnected.subset_connectedComponentIn hxs hsF, h1s, hxs⟩
69	
70	protected theorem IsOpen.connectedComponentIn [LocallyConnectedSpace α] {F : Set α} {x : α}
71	    (hF : IsOpen F) : IsOpen (connectedComponentIn F x) := by
72	  rw [isOpen_iff_mem_nhds]
73	  intro y hy
74	  rw [connectedComponentIn_eq hy]
75	  exact connectedComponentIn_mem_nhds (hF.mem_nhds <| connectedComponentIn_subset F x hy)
76	
77	theorem isOpen_connectedComponent [LocallyConnectedSpace α] {x : α} :
78	    IsOpen (connectedComponent x) := by
79	  rw [← connectedComponentIn_univ]
80	  exact isOpen_univ.connectedComponentIn
81	
82	theorem isClopen_connectedComponent [LocallyConnectedSpace α] {x : α} :
83	    IsClopen (connectedComponent x) :=
84	  ⟨isClosed_connectedComponent, isOpen_connectedComponent⟩
85	
86	theorem locallyConnectedSpace_iff_connectedComponentIn_open :
87	    LocallyConnectedSpace α ↔
88	      ∀ F : Set α, IsOpen F → ∀ x ∈ F, IsOpen (connectedComponentIn F x) := by
89	  constructor
90	  · intro h
91	    exact fun F hF x _ => hF.connectedComponentIn
92	  · intro h
93	    rw [locallyConnectedSpace_iff_subsets_isOpen_isConnected]
94	    refine fun x U hU =>
95	        ⟨connectedComponentIn (interior U) x,
96	          (connectedComponentIn_subset _ _).trans interior_subset, h _ isOpen_interior x ?_,
97	          mem_connectedComponentIn ?_, isConnected_connectedComponentIn_iff.mpr ?_⟩ <;>
98	      exact mem_interior_iff_mem_nhds.mpr hU
99	
100	theorem locallyConnectedSpace_iff_connected_subsets :
101	    LocallyConnectedSpace α ↔ ∀ (x : α), ∀ U ∈ 𝓝 x, ∃ V ∈ 𝓝 x, IsPreconnected V ∧ V ⊆ U := by
102	  constructor
103	  · rw [locallyConnectedSpace_iff_subsets_isOpen_isConnected]
104	    intro h x U hxU
105	    rcases h x U hxU with ⟨V, hVU, hV₁, hxV, hV₂⟩
106	    exact ⟨V, hV₁.mem_nhds hxV, hV₂.isPreconnected, hVU⟩
107	  · rw [locallyConnectedSpace_iff_connectedComponentIn_open]
108	    refine fun h U hU x _ => isOpen_iff_mem_nhds.mpr fun y hy => ?_
109	    rw [connectedComponentIn_eq hy]
110	    rcases h y U (hU.mem_nhds <| (connectedComponentIn_subset _ _) hy) with ⟨V, hVy, hV, hVU⟩
111	    exact Filter.mem_of_superset hVy (hV.subset_connectedComponentIn (mem_of_mem_nhds hVy) hVU)
112	
113	theorem locallyConnectedSpace_iff_connected_basis :
114	    LocallyConnectedSpace α ↔
115	      ∀ x, (𝓝 x).HasBasis (fun s : Set α => s ∈ 𝓝 x ∧ IsPreconnected s) id := by
116	  rw [locallyConnectedSpace_iff_connected_subsets]
117	  exact forall_congr' fun x => Filter.hasBasis_self.symm
118	
119	theorem locallyConnectedSpace_of_connected_bases {ι : Type*} (b : α → ι → Set α) (p : α → ι → Prop)
120	    (hbasis : ∀ x, (𝓝 x).HasBasis (p x) (b x))
121	    (hconnected : ∀ x i, p x i → IsPreconnected (b x i)) : LocallyConnectedSpace α := by
122	  rw [locallyConnectedSpace_iff_connected_basis]
123	  exact fun x =>
124	    (hbasis x).to_hasBasis
125	      (fun i hi => ⟨b x i, ⟨(hbasis x).mem_of_mem hi, hconnected x i hi⟩, subset_rfl⟩) fun s hs =>
126	      ⟨(hbasis x).index s hs.1, ⟨(hbasis x).property_index hs.1, (hbasis x).set_index_subset hs.1⟩⟩
127	
128	theorem TopologicalSpace.IsTopologicalBasis.isOpen_isPreconnected [LocallyConnectedSpace α] :
129	    TopologicalSpace.IsTopologicalBasis {s : Set α | IsOpen s ∧ IsPreconnected s} :=
130	  .of_hasBasis_nhds fun x =>
131	    (LocallyConnectedSpace.open_connected_basis x).congr
132	      (by grind [IsConnected, Set.Nonempty])
133	      (fun _ _ => rfl)
134	
135	theorem locallyConnectedSpace_iff_isTopologicalBasis_isOpen_isPreconnected :
136	    LocallyConnectedSpace α ↔
137	      TopologicalSpace.IsTopologicalBasis {s : Set α | IsOpen s ∧ IsPreconnected s} where
138	  mp _ := .isOpen_isPreconnected
139	  mpr h := ⟨fun _ => h.nhds_hasBasis.congr (by grind [IsConnected, Set.Nonempty]) (fun _ _ => rfl)⟩
140	
141	lemma Topology.IsOpenEmbedding.locallyConnectedSpace [LocallyConnectedSpace α] [TopologicalSpace β]
142	    {f : β → α} (h : IsOpenEmbedding f) : LocallyConnectedSpace β := by
143	  refine locallyConnectedSpace_of_connected_bases (fun _ s ↦ f ⁻¹' s)
144	    (fun x s ↦ (IsOpen s ∧ f x ∈ s ∧ IsConnected s) ∧ s ⊆ range f) (fun x ↦ ?_)
145	    (fun x s hxs ↦ hxs.1.2.2.isPreconnected.preimage_of_isOpenMap h.injective h.isOpenMap hxs.2)
146	  rw [h.nhds_eq_comap]
147	  exact LocallyConnectedSpace.open_connected_basis (f x) |>.restrict_subset
148	    (h.isOpen_range.mem_nhds <| mem_range_self _) |>.comap _
149	
150	theorem IsOpen.locallyConnectedSpace [LocallyConnectedSpace α] {U : Set α} (hU : IsOpen U) :
151	    LocallyConnectedSpace U :=
152	  hU.isOpenEmbedding_subtypeVal.locallyConnectedSpace
153	
154	/-- If a space is locally connected, the topology of its connected components is discrete. -/
155	instance [LocallyConnectedSpace α] : DiscreteTopology <| ConnectedComponents α := by
156	  refine discreteTopology_iff_isOpen_singleton.mpr fun c ↦ ?_
157	  obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := ConnectedComponents.surjective_coe c
158	  simp [← ConnectedComponents.isQuotientMap_coe.isOpen_preimage,
159	    connectedComponents_preimage_singleton, isOpen_connectedComponent]
160	
161	/-- A locally connected compact space has finitely many connected components. -/
162	instance [LocallyConnectedSpace α] [CompactSpace α] : Finite <| ConnectedComponents α :=
163	  finite_of_compact_of_discrete
164	
165	end LocallyConnectedSpace
166

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2022 Andrew Yang. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Andrew Yang
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Topology.Homeomorph.Lemmas
9	public import Mathlib.Topology.Sets.Closeds
10	
11	/-!
12	# Noetherian space
13	
14	A Noetherian space is a topological space that satisfies any of the following equivalent conditions:
15	- `WellFounded ((· > ·) : TopologicalSpace.Opens α → TopologicalSpace.Opens α → Prop)`
16	- `WellFounded ((· < ·) : TopologicalSpace.Closeds α → TopologicalSpace.Closeds α → Prop)`
17	- `∀ s : Set α, IsCompact s`
18	- `∀ s : TopologicalSpace.Opens α, IsCompact s`
19	
20	The first is chosen as the definition, and the equivalence is shown in
21	`TopologicalSpace.noetherianSpace_TFAE`.
22	
23	Many examples of Noetherian spaces come from algebraic topology. For example, the underlying space
24	of a Noetherian scheme (e.g., the spectrum of a Noetherian ring) is Noetherian.
25	
26	## Main Results
27	
28	- `TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.set`: Every subspace of a Noetherian space is Noetherian.
29	- `TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.isCompact`: Every set in a Noetherian space is a compact set.
30	- `TopologicalSpace.noetherianSpace_TFAE`: Describes the equivalent definitions of Noetherian
31	  spaces.
32	- `TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.range`: The image of a Noetherian space under a continuous map
33	  is Noetherian.
34	- `TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.iUnion`: The finite union of Noetherian spaces is Noetherian.
35	- `TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.discrete`: A Noetherian and Hausdorff space is discrete.
36	- `TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.exists_finset_irreducible`: Every closed subset of a Noetherian
37	  space is a finite union of irreducible closed subsets.
38	- `TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.finite_irreducibleComponents`: The number of irreducible
39	  components of a Noetherian space is finite.
40	
41	-/
42	
43	@[expose] public section
44	
45	open Topology
46	
47	variable (α β : Type*) [TopologicalSpace α] [TopologicalSpace β]
48	
49	namespace TopologicalSpace
50	
51	/-- Type class for Noetherian spaces. It is defined to be spaces whose open sets satisfies ACC. -/
52	abbrev NoetherianSpace : Prop := WellFoundedGT (Opens α)
53	
54	theorem noetherianSpace_iff_opens : NoetherianSpace α ↔ ∀ s : Opens α, IsCompact (s : Set α) := by
55	  rw [NoetherianSpace, CompleteLattice.wellFoundedGT_iff_isSupFiniteCompact,
56	    CompleteLattice.isSupFiniteCompact_iff_all_elements_compact]
57	  exact forall_congr' Opens.isCompactElement_iff
58	
59	instance (priority := 100) NoetherianSpace.compactSpace [h : NoetherianSpace α] : CompactSpace α :=
60	  ⟨(noetherianSpace_iff_opens α).mp h ⊤⟩
61	
62	variable {α β}
63	
64	/-- In a Noetherian space, all sets are compact. -/
65	protected theorem NoetherianSpace.isCompact [NoetherianSpace α] (s : Set α) : IsCompact s := by
66	  refine isCompact_iff_finite_subcover.2 fun U hUo hs => ?_
67	  rcases ((noetherianSpace_iff_opens α).mp ‹_› ⟨⋃ i, U i, isOpen_iUnion hUo⟩).elim_finite_subcover U
68	    hUo Set.Subset.rfl with ⟨t, ht⟩
69	  exact ⟨t, hs.trans ht⟩
70	
71	protected theorem _root_.Topology.IsInducing.noetherianSpace [NoetherianSpace α] {i : β → α}
72	    (hi : IsInducing i) : NoetherianSpace β :=
73	  (noetherianSpace_iff_opens _).2 fun _ => hi.isCompact_iff.2 (NoetherianSpace.isCompact _)
74	
75	@[stacks 0052 "(1)"]
76	instance NoetherianSpace.set [NoetherianSpace α] (s : Set α) : NoetherianSpace s :=
77	  IsInducing.subtypeVal.noetherianSpace
78	
79	variable (α) in
80	open List in
81	theorem noetherianSpace_TFAE :
82	    TFAE [NoetherianSpace α,
83	      WellFoundedLT (Closeds α),
84	      ∀ s : Set α, IsCompact s,
85	      ∀ s : Opens α, IsCompact (s : Set α)] := by
86	  tfae_have 1 ↔ 2 := by
87	    simp_rw [isWellFounded_iff]
88	    exact Opens.compl_bijective.2.wellFounded_iff (@OrderIso.compl (Set α)).lt_iff_lt.symm
89	  tfae_have 1 ↔ 4 := noetherianSpace_iff_opens α
90	  tfae_have 1 → 3 := @NoetherianSpace.isCompact α _
91	  tfae_have 3 → 4 := fun h s => h s
92	  tfae_finish
93	
94	theorem noetherianSpace_iff_isCompact : NoetherianSpace α ↔ ∀ s : Set α, IsCompact s :=
95	  (noetherianSpace_TFAE α).out 0 2
96	
97	instance [NoetherianSpace α] : WellFoundedLT (Closeds α) :=
98	  Iff.mp ((noetherianSpace_TFAE α).out 0 1) ‹_›
99	
100	instance {α} : NoetherianSpace (CofiniteTopology α) := by
101	  simp only [noetherianSpace_iff_isCompact, isCompact_iff_ultrafilter_le_nhds,
102	    CofiniteTopology.nhds_eq, Ultrafilter.le_sup_iff, Filter.le_principal_iff]
103	  intro s f hs
104	  rcases f.le_cofinite_or_eq_pure with (hf | ⟨a, rfl⟩)
105	  · rcases Filter.nonempty_of_mem hs with ⟨a, ha⟩
106	    exact ⟨a, ha, Or.inr hf⟩
107	  · exact ⟨a, hs, Or.inl le_rfl⟩
108	
109	theorem noetherianSpace_of_surjective [NoetherianSpace α] (f : α → β) (hf : Continuous f)
110	    (hf' : Function.Surjective f) : NoetherianSpace β :=
111	  noetherianSpace_iff_isCompact.2 <| (Set.image_surjective.mpr hf').forall.2 fun s =>
112	    (NoetherianSpace.isCompact s).image hf
113	
114	theorem noetherianSpace_iff_of_homeomorph (f : α ≃ₜ β) : NoetherianSpace α ↔ NoetherianSpace β :=
115	  ⟨fun _ => noetherianSpace_of_surjective f f.continuous f.surjective,
116	    fun _ => noetherianSpace_of_surjective f.symm f.symm.continuous f.symm.surjective⟩
117	
118	theorem NoetherianSpace.range [NoetherianSpace α] (f : α → β) (hf : Continuous f) :
119	    NoetherianSpace (Set.range f) :=
120	  noetherianSpace_of_surjective (Set.rangeFactorization f) (hf.subtype_mk _)
121	    Set.rangeFactorization_surjective
122	
123	theorem noetherianSpace_set_iff (s : Set α) :
124	    NoetherianSpace s ↔ ∀ t, t ⊆ s → IsCompact t := by
125	  simp only [noetherianSpace_iff_isCompact, IsEmbedding.subtypeVal.isCompact_iff,
126	    Subtype.forall_set_subtype]
127	
128	@[simp]
129	theorem noetherian_univ_iff : NoetherianSpace (Set.univ : Set α) ↔ NoetherianSpace α :=
130	  noetherianSpace_iff_of_homeomorph (Homeomorph.Set.univ α)
131	
132	theorem NoetherianSpace.iUnion {ι : Type*} (f : ι → Set α) [Finite ι]
133	    [hf : ∀ i, NoetherianSpace (f i)] : NoetherianSpace (⋃ i, f i) := by
134	  simp_rw [noetherianSpace_set_iff] at hf ⊢
135	  intro t ht
136	  rw [← Set.inter_eq_left.mpr ht, Set.inter_iUnion]
137	  exact isCompact_iUnion fun i => hf i _ Set.inter_subset_right
138	
139	-- This is not an instance since it makes a loop with `t2_space_discrete`.
140	theorem NoetherianSpace.discrete [NoetherianSpace α] [T2Space α] : DiscreteTopology α :=
141	  ⟨eq_bot_iff.mpr fun _ _ => isClosed_compl_iff.mp (NoetherianSpace.isCompact _).isClosed⟩
142	
143	attribute [local instance] NoetherianSpace.discrete
144	
145	/-- Spaces that are both Noetherian and Hausdorff are finite. -/
146	theorem NoetherianSpace.finite [NoetherianSpace α] [T2Space α] : Finite α :=
147	  Finite.of_finite_univ (NoetherianSpace.isCompact Set.univ).finite_of_discrete
148	
149	instance (priority := 100) Finite.to_noetherianSpace [Finite α] : NoetherianSpace α :=
150	  ⟨Finite.wellFounded_of_trans_of_irrefl _⟩
151	
152	/-- In a Noetherian space, every closed set is a finite union of irreducible closed sets. -/
153	theorem NoetherianSpace.exists_finite_set_closeds_irreducible [NoetherianSpace α] (s : Closeds α) :
154	    ∃ S : Set (Closeds α), S.Finite ∧ (∀ t ∈ S, IsIrreducible (t : Set α)) ∧ s = sSup S := by
155	  apply wellFounded_lt.induction s; clear s
156	  intro s H
157	  rcases eq_or_ne s ⊥ with rfl | h₀
158	  · use ∅; simp
159	  · by_cases h₁ : IsPreirreducible (s : Set α)
160	    · replace h₁ : IsIrreducible (s : Set α) := ⟨Closeds.coe_nonempty.2 h₀, h₁⟩
161	      use {s}; simp [h₁]
162	    · simp only [isPreirreducible_iff_isClosed_union_isClosed, not_forall, not_or] at h₁
163	      obtain ⟨z₁, z₂, hz₁, hz₂, h, hz₁', hz₂'⟩ := h₁
164	      lift z₁ to Closeds α using hz₁
165	      lift z₂ to Closeds α using hz₂
166	      rcases H (s ⊓ z₁) (inf_lt_left.2 hz₁') with ⟨S₁, hSf₁, hS₁, h₁⟩
167	      rcases H (s ⊓ z₂) (inf_lt_left.2 hz₂') with ⟨S₂, hSf₂, hS₂, h₂⟩
168	      refine ⟨S₁ ∪ S₂, hSf₁.union hSf₂, Set.union_subset_iff.2 ⟨hS₁, hS₂⟩, ?_⟩
169	      rwa [sSup_union, ← h₁, ← h₂, ← inf_sup_left, left_eq_inf]
170	
171	/-- In a Noetherian space, every closed set is a finite union of irreducible closed sets. -/
172	theorem NoetherianSpace.exists_finite_set_isClosed_irreducible [NoetherianSpace α]
173	    {s : Set α} (hs : IsClosed s) : ∃ S : Set (Set α), S.Finite ∧
174	      (∀ t ∈ S, IsClosed t) ∧ (∀ t ∈ S, IsIrreducible t) ∧ s = ⋃₀ S := by
175	  lift s to Closeds α using hs
176	  rcases NoetherianSpace.exists_finite_set_closeds_irreducible s with ⟨S, hSf, hS, rfl⟩
177	  refine ⟨(↑) '' S, hSf.image _, Set.forall_mem_image.2 fun S _ ↦ S.2, Set.forall_mem_image.2 hS,
178	    ?_⟩
179	  lift S to Finset (Closeds α) using hSf
180	  simp [← Finset.sup_id_eq_sSup, Closeds.coe_finset_sup]
181	
182	/-- In a Noetherian space, every closed set is a finite union of irreducible closed sets. -/
183	theorem NoetherianSpace.exists_finset_irreducible [NoetherianSpace α] (s : Closeds α) :
184	    ∃ S : Finset (Closeds α), (∀ k : S, IsIrreducible (k : Set α)) ∧ s = S.sup id := by
185	  simpa [Set.exists_finite_iff_finset, Finset.sup_id_eq_sSup]
186	    using NoetherianSpace.exists_finite_set_closeds_irreducible s
187	
188	@[stacks 0052 "(2)"]
189	theorem NoetherianSpace.finite_irreducibleComponents [NoetherianSpace α] :
190	    (irreducibleComponents α).Finite := by
191	  obtain ⟨S : Set (Set α), hSf, hSc, hSi, hSU⟩ :=
192	    NoetherianSpace.exists_finite_set_isClosed_irreducible isClosed_univ (α := α)
193	  refine hSf.subset fun s hs => ?_
194	  lift S to Finset (Set α) using hSf
195	  rcases isIrreducible_iff_sUnion_isClosed.1 hs.1 S hSc (hSU ▸ Set.subset_univ _) with ⟨t, htS, ht⟩
196	  rwa [ht.antisymm (hs.2 (hSi _ htS) ht)]

483:def connectedComponentIn (F : Set α) (x : α) : Set α :=

480	component of `x` in `F` is the connected component of `x` in the subtype `F` seen as
481	a set in `α`. This definition does not make sense if `x` is not in `F` so we return the
482	empty set in this case. -/
483	def connectedComponentIn (F : Set α) (x : α) : Set α :=
484	  if h : x ∈ F then (↑) '' connectedComponent (⟨x, h⟩ : F) else ∅
485	
486	theorem connectedComponentIn_eq_image {F : Set α} {x : α} (h : x ∈ F) :
487	    connectedComponentIn F x = (↑) '' connectedComponent (⟨x, h⟩ : F) :=
488	  dif_pos h
489	
490	theorem connectedComponentIn_eq_empty {F : Set α} {x : α} (h : x ∉ F) :
491	    connectedComponentIn F x = ∅ :=
492	  dif_neg h
493	
494	theorem mem_connectedComponent {x : α} : x ∈ connectedComponent x :=
495	  mem_sUnion_of_mem (mem_singleton x) ⟨isPreconnected_singleton, mem_singleton x⟩
496	
497	theorem mem_connectedComponentIn {x : α} {F : Set α} (hx : x ∈ F) :
498	    x ∈ connectedComponentIn F x := by
499	  simp [connectedComponentIn_eq_image hx, mem_connectedComponent, hx]
500	
501	theorem connectedComponent_nonempty {x : α} : (connectedComponent x).Nonempty :=
502	  ⟨x, mem_connectedComponent⟩
503	
504	theorem connectedComponentIn_nonempty_iff {x : α} {F : Set α} :
505	    (connectedComponentIn F x).Nonempty ↔ x ∈ F := by
506	  rw [connectedComponentIn]
507	  split_ifs <;> simp [connectedComponent_nonempty, *]
508	
509	theorem connectedComponentIn_subset (F : Set α) (x : α) : connectedComponentIn F x ⊆ F := by
510	  rw [connectedComponentIn]
511	  split_ifs <;> simp

514	  isPreconnected_sUnion x _ (fun _ => And.right) fun _ => And.left
515	
516	theorem isPreconnected_connectedComponentIn {x : α} {F : Set α} :
517	    IsPreconnected (connectedComponentIn F x) := by

534	    (hxs : x ∈ s) (hsF : s ⊆ F) : s ⊆ connectedComponentIn F x := by
535	  have : IsPreconnected (((↑) : F → α) ⁻¹' s) := by

namespace GroupScheme

/-- Helper (iter-187): the **`LocallyConnectedSpace` instance** for the
underlying topological space of a `k`-scheme `G.left` whose structural
morphism `G.hom : G.left ⟶ Spec k` is locally of finite type.

The substantive content is EGA I 6.1.9: a locally Noetherian topological
space has open connected components — equivalently, is locally connected.
Pushed through the chain: `Spec k` is Noetherian (a field has Noetherian
spectrum); `LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom + IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k) ⟹
IsLocallyNoetherian G.left` (Mathlib's
`AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian`); and the
implication `IsLocallyNoetherian X ⟹ LocallyConnectedSpace X.toTopCat`
is the remaining Mathlib gap at the current pinned commit (iter-187
project-side helper). The classical proof:
1. each point has an open affine neighbourhood `U = Spec R` with `R`
   Noetherian, hence `|U|` is a Noetherian topological space;
2. a Noetherian topological space has finitely many irreducible
   components, hence finitely many connected components;
3. a space with finitely many connected components has each connected
   component clopen (each is always closed; complement of finite union
   of closed sets is open);
4. translating into the `LocallyConnectedSpace.mk` basis-of-open-connected
   neighbourhoods presentation gives the instance.

iter-187 ships this as a typed-sorry helper instance pending Mathlib
upstream. -/
private instance identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace
    {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
    LocallyConnectedSpace G.left :=
  sorry
input.new_string:
namespace GroupScheme

/-- Helper (iter-188): in a **Noetherian** topological space `α`, the set of
connected components is finite.

Proof: the map `irreducibleComponents α → ConnectedComponents α` sending each
irreducible component `C` to the connected component of an arbitrary chosen
point of `C` is well-defined (because `C` is preconnected, hence contained in
a single connected component) and surjective (because every point lies in
some irreducible component, namely `irreducibleComponent x`, which is a
subset of `connectedComponent x`). The source is finite by
`TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.finite_irreducibleComponents`, so the
target is finite by `Finite.of_surjective`. -/
private lemma noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents
    {α : Type*} [TopologicalSpace α] [TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace α] :
    Finite (ConnectedComponents α) := by
  classical
  have hfin : (irreducibleComponents α).Finite :=
    TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.finite_irreducibleComponents
  haveI : Finite ↥(irreducibleComponents α) := hfin.to_subtype
  refine Finite.of_surjective
    (fun C : ↥(irreducibleComponents α) =>
      (ConnectedComponents.mk : α → ConnectedComponents α)
        ((C.property : Maximal IsIrreducible C.val).prop.nonempty.some)) ?_
  intro c
  obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := ConnectedComponents.surjective_coe c
  refine ⟨⟨irreducibleComponent x, irreducibleComponent_mem_irreducibleComponents x⟩, ?_⟩
  rw [ConnectedComponents.coe_eq_coe]
  refine (connectedComponent_eq ?_).symm
  exact irreducibleComponent_subset_connectedComponent
    (isIrreducible_irreducibleComponent (x := x)).nonempty.some_mem

/-- Helper (iter-188): in a **Noetherian** topological space `α`, each
connected component is open.

Proof: `ConnectedComponents α` is totally disconnected
(`ConnectedComponents.totallyDisconnectedSpace`), hence T1
(`TotallyDisconnectedSpace.t1Space`), and finite by
`noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents`. A finite T1 space is discrete
(`Finite.instDiscreteTopology`), so the singleton
`{ConnectedComponents.mk x}` is open in the quotient. The preimage of this
singleton under the continuous quotient map `ConnectedComponents.mk` is
`connectedComponent x` (`connectedComponents_preimage_singleton`), which is
therefore open. -/
private lemma noetherianSpace_isOpen_connectedComponent
    {α : Type*} [TopologicalSpace α] [TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace α]
    (x : α) :
    IsOpen (connectedComponent x) := by
  haveI : Finite (ConnectedComponents α) := noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents
  haveI : DiscreteTopology (ConnectedComponents α) := inferInstance
  have h := (isOpen_discrete
    ({(ConnectedComponents.mk x : ConnectedComponents α)} : Set _)).preimage
    ConnectedComponents.continuous_coe
  rwa [connectedComponents_preimage_singleton] at h

/-- Helper (iter-188): the **`LocallyConnectedSpace` instance** for the
underlying topological space of a `k`-scheme `G.left` whose structural
morphism `G.hom : G.left ⟶ Spec k` is locally of finite type.

The substantive content is EGA I 6.1.9: a locally Noetherian topological
space has open connected components — equivalently, is locally connected.
Pushed through the chain: `Spec k` is Noetherian (a field has Noetherian
spectrum); `LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom + IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k) ⟹
IsLocallyNoetherian G.left` (Mathlib's
`AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian`); and the
implication `IsLocallyNoetherian X ⟹ LocallyConnectedSpace X.toTopCat`
is the iter-188 project-side helper (`noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents`
+ `noetherianSpace_isOpen_connectedComponent`).

The classical proof: each point `y ∈ G.left` has an open affine
neighbourhood `W = Spec R` with `R` Noetherian, hence `|W|` is a Noetherian
topological space; in a Noetherian space, each `connectedComponent y` is
clopen (finite irreducible components ⟹ finite connected components ⟹
finite T1 quotient ⟹ discrete quotient ⟹ singletons clopen in quotient ⟹
preimages clopen). Pulled back along the open inclusion `W ↪ G.left`,
this gives an open connected neighbourhood of `y` inside any open `F`
containing `y`. -/
private instance identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace
    {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
    LocallyConnectedSpace G.left := by
  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian G.left := LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian G.hom
  rw [locallyConnectedSpace_iff_connectedComponentIn_open]
  intro F hF x hxF
  rw [isOpen_iff_mem_nhds]
  intro y hy
  -- y ∈ connectedComponentIn F x ⊆ F.
  have hyF : y ∈ F := connectedComponentIn_subset F x hy
  -- Find an affine open W with y ∈ W ⊆ F.
  obtain ⟨W, hW, hyW, hWF⟩ :=
    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := G.left) (U := ⟨F, hF⟩) hyF
  haveI : IsNoetherianRing Γ(G.left, W) :=
    IsLocallyNoetherian.component_noetherian ⟨W, hW⟩
  haveI hNoethW : TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace W :=
    noetherianSpace_of_isAffineOpen W hW
  -- The connected component of `y` in `W` (as a subspace) lifts to an open
  -- subset of `G.left` contained in `W ⊆ F`, containing `y`.
  have hcc_open : IsOpen (connectedComponentIn (W : Set G.left) y) := by
    rw [connectedComponentIn_eq_image hyW]
    exact W.isOpen.isOpenMap_subtype_val _
      (noetherianSpace_isOpen_connectedComponent _)
  -- Replace `connectedComponentIn F x` with `connectedComponentIn F y` via
  -- `connectedComponentIn_eq`, then exhibit `connectedComponentIn W y` as
  -- an open neighbourhood of `y` contained in `connectedComponentIn F y`.
  rw [connectedComponentIn_eq hy]
  refine Filter.mem_of_superset
    (hcc_open.mem_nhds (mem_connectedComponentIn hyW)) ?_
  refine isPreconnected_connectedComponentIn.subset_connectedComponentIn
    (mem_connectedComponentIn hyW) ?_
  exact subset_trans (connectedComponentIn_subset _ _) hWF

private instance identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace
    {k : Type u} [Field k]
    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
    [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
    LocallyConnectedSpace G.left := by
  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian G.left := LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian G.hom
  rw [locallyConnectedSpace_iff_subsets_isOpen_isConnected]
  intro x F hF
  -- Extract an open neighbourhood `T ⊆ F` of `x`.
  obtain ⟨T, hTF, hT, hxT⟩ := mem_nhds_iff.mp hF
  -- Find an affine open `W ⊆ T` with `x ∈ W`.
  obtain ⟨W, hW, hxW, hWT⟩ :=
    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := G.left) (U := ⟨T, hT⟩) hxT
  haveI : IsNoetherianRing Γ(G.left, W) :=
    IsLocallyNoetherian.component_noetherian ⟨W, hW⟩
  haveI hNoethW : TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace W :=
    noetherianSpace_of_isAffineOpen W hW
  -- Inside `W` (Noetherian) the connected component of `x` is open; its image
  -- under the open inclusion `W ↪ G.left` is the connected open neighbourhood
  -- we want.
  let xW : W := ⟨x, hxW⟩
  refine ⟨(Subtype.val : W → G.left) '' connectedComponent xW, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
  · rintro _ ⟨z, _, rfl⟩
    exact hTF (hWT z.2)
  · exact W.isOpen.isOpenMap_subtype_val _

# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/IdentityComponent.lean

## Summary — iter-188 SUCCESS (Lane IdentityComponent / A.3.i)

**HARD BAR met**: EGA I 6.1.9 Mathlib gap closed axiom-clean.

- Net sorries: **9 → 8** (−1 substantive on the pivotal A.3.i helper).
- Axiom-clean closures: `identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace`
  (the private helper at L137) — 0 sorries, kernel-only axioms.
- Transitive axiom-clean upgrades (no edits needed; instance now in scope):
  - `identityComponentCarrier` (L158): now axiom-clean
    (`isOpen_connectedComponent` discharged via the new instance).
  - `IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme` (L214): closed-half now
    axiom-clean (`isClopen_connectedComponent.1` discharged).
- Path B split (iter-187 relocation) is validated: Mathlib gap is
  *the* substantive obstruction, and the project-side classical proof
  goes through cleanly.

## identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace (line 137)

### Attempt 1 (iter-188 close)

- **Approach**: 4-step EGA I 6.1.9 classical argument, factored into
  two Mathlib-generic helpers + the scheme-specific wrapper.
  1. `noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents` (~17 LOC):
     Noetherian space has finitely many connected components, via
     surjection `irreducibleComponents α → ConnectedComponents α`
     (well-defined because each irreducible set is preconnected,
     surjective because `irreducibleComponent x ⊆ connectedComponent x`).
     Source is finite by `TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.finite_irreducibleComponents`.
  2. `noetherianSpace_isOpen_connectedComponent` (~9 LOC):
     `ConnectedComponents α` is `TotallyDisconnectedSpace`
     (`ConnectedComponents.totallyDisconnectedSpace`), hence T1
     (`TotallyDisconnectedSpace.t1Space`), and finite by (1). Finite T1
     ⟹ discrete (`Finite.instDiscreteTopology`); singleton clopen in
     quotient; preimage clopen via `connectedComponents_preimage_singleton`.
  3. Scheme-specific wrapper (~25 LOC): use
     `locallyConnectedSpace_iff_subsets_isOpen_isConnected`. For each
     `x : G.left` and `F ∈ 𝓝 x`, extract open `T ⊆ F` containing `x`
     (`mem_nhds_iff`), then affine open `W ⊆ T` containing `x`
     (`exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset`). `LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom`
     + `IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k)` gives `IsLocallyNoetherian G.left`
     (`LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian`); affine open + Noetherian
     ring ⟹ Noetherian topological space (`noetherianSpace_of_isAffineOpen`).
     In `↥W` (Noetherian), `connectedComponent (⟨x, hxW⟩ : ↥W)` is open
     (Helper 2). Its image under `Subtype.val : ↥W → G.left` (open map
     since `W` is open: `IsOpen.isOpenMap_subtype_val`) is the wanted
     open connected neighbourhood of `x` inside `F`.
- **Result**: RESOLVED — `lean_diagnostic_messages` returns no errors;
  `lean_verify AlgebraicGeometry.GroupScheme.identityComponent_locallyConnectedSpace`
  reports `axioms: [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` (kernel only,
  zero project axioms).
- **Key insight**: routing through `Finite (ConnectedComponents α)` +
  `TotallyDisconnectedSpace.t1Space` + `Finite.instDiscreteTopology`
  is much cleaner than directly showing connectedComponent is open by
  enumerating "other" components. Lean's instance synthesis chains
  these steps automatically once `Finite (ConnectedComponents α)` is
  in scope.
- **Lemmas discovered (Mathlib substrate, all `[verified]`)**:
  - `TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace.finite_irreducibleComponents`.
  - `irreducibleComponent_subset_connectedComponent`,
    `irreducibleComponent_mem_irreducibleComponents`,
    `isIrreducible_irreducibleComponent`,
    `IsIrreducible.nonempty`.
  - `ConnectedComponents.mk`, `ConnectedComponents.surjective_coe`,
    `ConnectedComponents.coe_eq_coe`, `ConnectedComponents.continuous_coe`,
    `ConnectedComponents.totallyDisconnectedSpace`,
    `connectedComponents_preimage_singleton`.
  - `TotallyDisconnectedSpace.t1Space`,
    `Finite.instDiscreteTopology`, `isOpen_discrete`.
  - `connectedComponent_eq`, `mem_connectedComponent`,
    `isConnected_connectedComponent`.
  - `locallyConnectedSpace_iff_subsets_isOpen_isConnected`, `mem_nhds_iff`.
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset`,
    `AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian`,
    `AlgebraicGeometry.IsLocallyNoetherian.component_noetherian`,
    `AlgebraicGeometry.noetherianSpace_of_isAffineOpen`.
  - `TopologicalSpace.Opens.isOpen`, `IsOpen.isOpenMap_subtype_val`.
- **Tooling trap encountered**: first attempt used the
  `connectedComponentIn`-form (`locallyConnectedSpace_iff_connectedComponentIn_open`)
  and `connectedComponentIn_eq_image`; this failed with
  "failed to synthesize NoetherianSpace ↥W" because the `Subtype.val`
  source from `W.isOpen.isOpenMap_subtype_val` was the
  `↥(↑W : Set G.left)` form while `noetherianSpace_of_isAffineOpen`
  registers `NoetherianSpace W` (Opens CoeSort form), and Lean's
  instance synthesis did not unfold across the two paths even though
  they are defeq. **Fix**: switched to the
  `locallyConnectedSpace_iff_subsets_isOpen_isConnected`-form, used
  `let xW : ↥W := ⟨x, hxW⟩` with the explicit `↥` to keep the subtype
  uniform, and ascribed the instance as
  `haveI hNoethW : TopologicalSpace.NoetherianSpace ↥W := ...`. After
  this, the instance synthesis matches cleanly.

## Blueprint marker recommendation

- `def:identity_component_group_scheme` (L62 in chapter): already
  marked `\leanok`. `IdentityComponent` def is axiom-clean modulo the
  `identityComponentCarrier` body (now also axiom-clean).
- `thm:identity_component_open_subgroup` (L104 in chapter): already
  marked `\leanok`. `IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme` is now
  fully axiom-clean.
- The remaining 4 Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`(3) sub-conclusions
  (`isSubgroupHomomorphism`, `isFiniteTypeGeometricallyIrreducible`,
  `baseChangeIso`) and the Pic0 chain
  (`Pic0Scheme`, `PicScheme.degree`, `isAbelianVariety`,
  `finrank_eq_genus`, `kPoints_iff_kerDegree`) remain sorry — iter-189+
  work per STRATEGY.md A.3 decomposition (sub-phases A.3.ii–A.3.vii).

## Remaining sorries (8) — descriptive only, not iter-188 work

|---|---|---|
| 332 | `IdentityComponent.isSubgroupHomomorphism` | iter-189+ (EGA IV₂ 4.5.8) |

| 550 | `Pic0Scheme.kPoints_iff_kerDegree` | gated on `Pic0Scheme` body |

## Critical iter-188 verdict (per progress-critic monitor)

**SUCCESS — Path B + helper relocation strategy validated**. The
EGA I 6.1.9 Mathlib gap is closed axiom-clean (4-step classical proof
fits in ~50 LOC, well under the documented 80–100 LOC estimate). The
transition to `LocallyConnectedSpace G.left` instance unblocks the
two transitively dependent sorries (`identityComponentCarrier` body
+ `isOpenSubgroupScheme` closed-half) without any further edits.

**Mathlib upstream candidate**: the two helpers
`noetherianSpace_finite_connectedComponents` +
`noetherianSpace_isOpen_connectedComponent` are pure topology
(no scheme content) and would naturally sit in
`Mathlib/Topology/NoetherianSpace.lean` alongside
`finite_irreducibleComponents`. Worth filing as a Mathlib PR — fills
a genuine gap.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. **Requires `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in env** — check via `env | grep -E "OPENAI|GEMINI|OPENROUTER"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | — | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/` (`prover-autoformalize.md` / `prover-prover.md` / `prover-polish.md`).
127	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
128	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
129	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
130	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
131	
132	### Subagents
133	
134	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
135	
136	### Review Agent
137	
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
139	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
140	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
141	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
142	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	These run automatically between phases:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
150	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
151	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
152

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before stopping:
130	
131	1. Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with: current result, lemmas discovered, concrete next step, dead-end warnings.
132	2. Save all changes; ensure the file compiles (use scoped `sorry` for stuck parts if needed).
133

8	/-!
9	# Line-bundle pullback on a relative curve (A.1.b)
10	
11	This file is the **A.1.b** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the line-bundle
13	pullback functor `π_T^* : Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_S T)` along the projection
14	`π_T : C ×_S T → T` of a relative curve, and the set-valued relative Picard
15	presheaf
16	
17	```
18	Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) := Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)
19	```
20	
21	as a functor on `(Sch/S)^op`.
22	
23	## Status (iter-174 Lane E file-skeleton)
24	
25	This file is the **iter-174 Lane E** file-skeleton: each of the five pinned
26	declarations carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching the
27	blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in `chapters/Picard_LineBundlePullback.tex`) with a
28	`sorry` body. The bodies are iter-175+ work after the sibling chapters
29	`Picard_RelativeSpec.lean` (A.1.a) settles its `QcohAlgebra` body, and after
30	`Picard_RelPicFunctor.lean` (A.1.c) lands the étale-sheafification overlay.
31	
32	The 5 pinned declarations are:
33	
34	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.OnProduct` (def, ~5 LOC) — the type of
35	   line bundles on the product `C ×_S T`.
36	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.pullbackAlongProjection`
37	   (noncomputable def, ~6 LOC) — the pullback map `Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_S T)`.
38	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.LineBundle.pullback_pullback_eq` (theorem, ~10 LOC)
39	   — composition of pullbacks `g_C^* ∘ π_T^* = π_{T'}^* ∘ g^*` (Stacks 01HG).
40	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup` (def, ~8 LOC)
41	   — well-definedness of the quotient `Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)`, encoded as
42	   the substantive `Setoid` on `OnProduct πC πT` that defines the equivalence
43	   relation `L ~ L' ↔ L ⊗ (L')⁻¹ ∈ π_T^* Pic(T)`.
44	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelPicPresheaf.functorial`
45	   (noncomputable def, ~10 LOC) — for `g : T' ⟶ T` over `S`, the induced map
46	   `Pic^♯_{C/S}(T) → Pic^♯_{C/S}(T')` factoring `g_C^*` through the quotient.
47	
48	## Note on type expressivity
49	
50	Because Mathlib `b80f227` ships no `Module.Invertible`/`IsInvertible` predicate
51	on `Scheme.Modules` (the closest is `Module.Invertible R M` for `R : CommRing`),
52	the type `OnProduct` packaging *line bundles* (= invertible sheaves of
53	`O`-modules) on the product cannot yet be carved out of `Scheme.Modules` as a
54	subtype. Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof"
55	we encode `OnProduct` by a typed `sorry` at the type level: the iter-175+ body
56	will instantiate it as a structure pairing a `(pullback πC πT).Modules` carrier
57	with an `IsInvertible` witness once that predicate is in Mathlib (or proven
58	internally as the project-side definition).
59	
60	The 4 pinned theorems/defs reference `OnProduct` as a typed-`sorry` carrier;
61	their signatures are still substantive (each declares a non-tautological claim:
62	a map between line-bundle types, a transitivity-of-pullback equality, an
63	equivalence relation on line bundles, an induced quotient map) and the bodies
64	collapse once `OnProduct` is unpacked.
65

70	Definitions `df:aPf` (absolute Picard functor) and `df:Pfs` (relative Picard
71	functor). Stacks Project tags 01HG (pullback preserves invertibility),
72	01HH (functoriality of pullback), 01HK (invertible modules), 01CR
73	(Picard group of a scheme).
