417	\subsection*{The main \(\chi\)-identity}
418	
419	\begin{theorem}
420	
421	
422	\leanok
423	[Euler-characteristic identity for \(\mathcal O_C(D)\)]
424	  \label{thm:euler_char_eq_deg_plus_one_minus_genus}
425	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus}
426	  
427	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], IV.1, Theorem~1.3 (the reduction inside the
428	  % proof: ``Thus we have to show that for any \(D\), $\chi(\mathscr L(D))
429	  % = \deg D + 1 - g$'') (read from
430	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 312).
431	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Theorem 1.3 (Riemann--Roch). \emph{Let \(D\) be a
432	  % divisor on a curve \(X\) of genus \(g\). Then}
433	  % \(l(D) - l(K - D) = \deg D + 1 - g\)."
434	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Thus we have to show that for any \(D\),
435	  % \(\chi(\mathscr L(D)) = \deg D + 1 - g\),
436	  % where for any coherent sheaf \(\mathscr F\) on \(X\), \(\chi(\mathscr F)\)
437	  % is the Euler characteristic
438	  % \(\chi(\mathscr F) = \dim H^0(X, \mathscr F) - \dim H^1(X, \mathscr F)\)."
439	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, IV.1, Theorem~1.3, p.~295.}
440	  Let \(C\) be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over
441	  \(\bar k\) of genus \(g = g(C)\), and let \(D \in \mathrm{Div}(C)\). Then
442	  \[
443	    \chi(\mathcal O_C(D)) \;=\; \deg(D) + 1 - g.
444	  \]
445	\end{theorem}
446	
447	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "First we consider the case \(D = 0\). Then our
448	% formula says
449	% \(\dim H^0(X, \mathscr O_X) - \dim H^1(X, \mathscr O_X) = 1 - g\),
450	% because \(H^0(X, \mathscr O_X) = k\) for any projective variety
451	% (I, 3.4), and \(\dim H^1(X, \mathscr O_X) = g\) by (1.1).
452	% Next, let \(D\) be any divisor, and let \(P\) be any point. We will show
453	% that the formula is true for \(D\) if and only if it is true for
454	% \(D + P\). Since any divisor can be reached from \(0\) in a finite number
455	% of steps by adding or subtracting a point each time, this will show
456	% the result holds for all \(D\).
457	% We consider \(P\) as a closed subscheme of \(X\). Its structure sheaf is
458	% the skyscraper sheaf \(k\) sitting at the point \(P\), which we denote by
459	% \(k(P)\), and its ideal sheaf is \(\mathscr L(-P)\) by (II, 6.18).
460	% Therefore we have an exact sequence
461	% \(0 \to \mathscr L(-P) \to \mathscr O_X \to k(P) \to 0\).
462	% Tensoring with \(\mathscr L(D + P)\) we get
463	% \(0 \to \mathscr L(D) \to \mathscr L(D + P) \to k(P) \to 0\),
464	% (since \(\mathscr L(P)\) is locally free of rank 1, tensoring by it
465	% does not affect the sheaf \(k(P)\)). Now the Euler characteristic is
466	% additive on short exact sequences (III, Ex. 5.1), and $\chi(k(P))
467	% = 1$, so we have
468	% \(\chi(\mathscr L(D + P)) = \chi(\mathscr L(D)) + 1\).
469	% On the other hand, \(\deg D + P = \deg D + 1\), so our formula is true
470	% for \(D\) if and only if it is true for \(D + P\), as required."
471	\begin{proof}
472	  \leanok
473	  \uses{def:eulerChar_curve, def:l_invariant,
474	    lem:euler_char_shortExact_add, lem:euler_char_iso,
475	    lem:euler_char_skyscraperSheaf}
476	
477	  The proof is by induction on the structure of \(\mathrm{Div}(C)\) as the free
478	  abelian group on closed points (REF,
479	  REF). Both sides of the asserted
480	  equation are additive in \(D\) (the right-hand side by linearity of
481	  \(\deg\), REF; the left-hand side by the
482	  inductive step proved below), so it suffices to verify the formula
483	  for \(D = 0\) and to show that it transports across the elementary
484	  modification \(D \mapsto D + [P]\) at any closed point \(P \in C\).
485	
486	  \emph{Base case \(D = 0\).} The line bundle \(\mathcal O_C(0)\) is the
487	  structure sheaf \(\mathcal O_C\), and the formula
488	  \(\chi(\mathcal O_C) = 1 - g\) reads
489	  \[
490	    \dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C)
491	    - \dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal O_C)
492	    \;=\; 1 - g.
493	  \]
494	  The second term is the definition of the genus,
495	  \(\dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal O_C) = g\) (REF). The
496	  first term is \(\dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C) = 1\): \(C\) is a
497	  proper geometrically irreducible curve over the algebraically
498	  closed field \(\bar k\), so its connected components agree with its
499	  geometrically connected components, and $H^0(C, \mathcal O_C)
500	  \cong \bar k\( as \)\bar k$-vector spaces (the Hartshorne~I.3.4
501	  statement that ``\(H^0(X, \mathcal O_X) = k\) for any projective
502	  variety'' over an algebraically closed field; the Mathlib home is
503	  the project's \(H^0\)-bridge in
504	  \texttt{Cohomology\_StructureSheafModuleK.tex}). Substituting these
505	  two values gives \(1 - g = 1 - g\), as required.
506	
507	  \emph{Inductive step (Hartshorne's ``\(D \leftrightsquigarrow D + P\)''
508	  step).} Let \(P \in C\) be a closed point. We follow Hartshorne's
509	  argument: view \(P\) as a closed subscheme of \(C\); its structure
510	  sheaf is the skyscraper sheaf \(k(P) \cong \bar k\) supported at \(P\),
511	  and its sheaf of ideals is \(\mathcal O_C(-[P]) \subset \mathcal O_C\)
512	  (the locally principal ideal sheaf cutting out \(P\) in \(C\)). Hence
513	  the short exact sequence
514	  \[
515	    0 \to \mathcal O_C(-[P]) \to \mathcal O_C \to k(P) \to 0
516	  \]
517	  in \(\mathbf{Coh}(C)\) (Hartshorne~II.6.18). Tensoring with the
518	  invertible sheaf \(\mathcal O_C(D + [P])\) (a locally free
519	  \(\mathcal O_C\)-module of rank \(1\), so tensoring preserves exactness
520	  and leaves the skyscraper sheaf \(k(P)\) unchanged) yields
521	  \[
522	    0 \to \mathcal O_C(D)
523	    \to \mathcal O_C(D + [P])
524	    \to k(P) \to 0.
525	  \]
526	  (Strictly: tensoring \(\mathcal O_C(-[P]) \subset \mathcal O_C\) with
527	  \(\mathcal O_C(D + [P])\) produces a sheaf isomorphic — but not
528	  syntactically equal — to \(\mathcal O_C(D)\), and the skyscraper
529	  factor \(k(P)\) is preserved up to isomorphism because
530	  \(\mathcal O_C(D + [P])\) is locally free of rank \(1\). We transport
531	  along this isomorphism via iso-invariance of \(\chi\),
532	  REF.)
533	  Additivity of \(\chi\) on the short exact sequence
534	  (REF, the project-side
535	  factoring of the ``Now the Euler characteristic is additive on short
536	  exact sequences (III, Ex.~5.1)'' input of Hartshorne~IV.1.3) gives
537	  \[
538	    \chi(\mathcal O_C(D + [P]))
539	    \;=\;
540	    \chi(\mathcal O_C(D)) + \chi(k(P)).
541	  \]
542	  The skyscraper-sheaf input
543	  \(\chi(k(P)) = 1\) is the standalone substrate lemma
544	  REF (factored from the
545	  same Hartshorne~IV.1.3 line); its proof splits into the
546	  H\(^0\) identification \(\dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, k(P)) = 1\) and the
547	  H\(^1\) vanishing \(\dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, k(P)) = 0\), pursued
548	  independently by the prover (see the lemma's two-paragraph proof
549	  body). Therefore
550	  \[
551	    \chi(\mathcal O_C(D + [P])) \;=\; \chi(\mathcal O_C(D)) + 1.
552	  \]
553	  On the other hand, \(\deg([P]) = 1\) (every closed point contributes
554	  \(1\) to the bare-sum degree of REF), so by
555	  REF
556	  \[
557	    \deg(D + [P]) + 1 - g \;=\; \deg(D) + 1 - g + 1.
558	  \]
559	  Comparing the two displayed identities, the formula
560	  \(\chi(\mathcal O_C(D)) = \deg(D) + 1 - g\) holds for \(D\) if and only
561	  if it holds for \(D + [P]\). Since the closed-point divisors \([P]\)
562	  generate \(\mathrm{Div}(C)\) as a free abelian group, the formula propagates
563	  from \(D = 0\) to every \(D \in \mathrm{Div}(C)\) by induction on the (signed)
564	  number of generators in the expression of \(D\).
565	
566	  \emph{Lean reference note on the three substrate lemmas.} The
567	  inductive step of this proof consumes three substantive substrate
568	  lemmas, each pinned to its own Lean declaration in
569	  \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RRFormula.lean}: the additivity
570	  REF (\(\chi\) on a short
571	  exact sequence, gated on the project-side
572	  \(\Module \bar k\)-flavoured Ext-LES carrier + Grothendieck
573	  vanishing + six-term rank-counting), the iso-invariance
574	  REF (\(\chi\) on
575	  isomorphism classes, axiom-clean against Mathlib's
576	  \(\Ext\)-postcomposition API; closed iter-186), and the skyscraper
577	  identity REF
578	  (\(\chi(k(P)) = 1\), splitting into an H\(^0\) half closed against
579	  the project's existing \(H^0\)-bridge and an H\(^1\) half gated on
580	  Mathlib flasque-cohomology — see the lemma's annotated proof body).
581	  Mathlib \texttt{b80f227} provides the Euler-characteristic API
582	  \texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.EulerCharacteristic}
583	  (\texttt{HomologicalComplex.eulerChar},
584	  \texttt{GradedObject.eulerChar}) but does \emph{not} ship the
585	  specific additivity statement
586	  \texttt{CategoryTheory.ShortExact.eulerChar\_additive} at the
587	  \(\Module \bar k\)-flavoured sheaf-cohomology level the project
588	  consumes; the additivity lemma is the project-side bridge.
589	\end{proof}

591	\section{The Riemann--Roch formula in genus zero}
592	
593	\begin{theorem}
594	
595	
596	\leanok
597	[Riemann--Roch formula in genus zero]
598	  \label{thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero}
599	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.l_eq_degree_plus_one_of_genus_zero}
600	  
601	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], IV.1, Example~1.3.5 (genus-\(0\) specialisation
602	  % of Riemann--Roch; the curve-is-rational consequence) (read from
603	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 314).
604	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Example 1.3.5. Recall that a curve is \emph{rational}
605	  % if it is birational to \(\mathbf P^1\) (I, Ex. 6.1). Since curves in
606	  % this chapter are complete and nonsingular by definition, a curve
607	  % \(X\) is rational if and only if \(X \cong \mathbf P^1\) (I, 6.12). Now
608	  % using (1.3) we can show that \(X\) is rational if and only if \(g = 0\).
609	  % We already know that \(p_a(\mathbf P^1) = 0\) (I, Ex. 7.2), so
610	  % conversely suppose given a curve \(X\) of genus 0. Let \(P, Q\) be two
611	  % distinct points of \(X\) and apply Riemann--Roch to the divisor
612	  % \(D = P - Q\). Since \(\deg K - D = -2\), using (1.3.3) above, we have
613	  % \(l(K - D) = 0\), and so we find \(l(D) = 1\). But \(D\) is a divisor of
614	  % degree 0, so by (1.2) we have \(D \sim 0\), in other words $P \sim
615	  % Q\(. But this implies \)X$ is rational (II, 6.10.1)."
616	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, IV.1, Example~1.3.5, p.~297.}
617	  Let \(C\) be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over
618	  \(\bar k\) with \(g(C) = 0\), and let \(D \in \mathrm{Div}(C)\) with
619	  \(\deg(D) \geq 0\). Then
620	  \[
621	    \ell(D) \;=\; \deg(D) + 1.
622	  \]
623	\end{theorem}
624	
625	\begin{proof}
626	  \leanok
627	  
628	  Specialising
629	  REF to the genus-\(0\)
630	  hypothesis \(g = 0\) yields
631	  \[
632	    \chi(\mathcal O_C(D)) \;=\; \deg(D) + 1.
633	  \]
634	  Unfolding \(\chi\) via REF and the definition
635	  \(\ell(D) = \dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(D))\)
636	  (REF), this rewrites as
637	  \[
638	    \ell(D) - \dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(D))
639	    \;=\; \deg(D) + 1.
640	  \]
641	  Under the standing genus-\(0\) hypothesis and the assumption
642	  \(\deg(D) \geq 0\), the higher-cohomology term vanishes:
643	  \[
644	    H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(D)) \;=\; 0
645	    \qquad \text{for } \deg(D) \geq 0.
646	  \]
647	  Substituting yields \(\ell(D) = \deg(D) + 1\), as required.
648	
649	  \emph{The \(H^1\)-vanishing input.} On a smooth proper geometrically
650	  irreducible curve \(C / \bar k\) with \(g(C) = 0\), the vanishing
651	  \(H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(D)) = 0\) for \(\deg(D) \geq 0\) is the Hartshorne
652	  formulation Lemma~1.2 read covariantly: \(\ell(D) \neq 0\) forces
653	  \(\deg(D) \geq 0\); equivalently, for a curve where every
654	  degree-\(0\)-or-higher invertible sheaf is generated by global
655	  sections after a single point-twist, \(H^1\) of the twist must vanish
656	  by the long exact sequence of the skyscraper extension at any
657	  closed point. In the chain of sub-build chapters, this \(H^1\)
658	  vanishing is the natural content of \texttt{RR.3} (sibling chapter
659	  \texttt{RiemannRoch\_OcOfD.tex}, to be added), where the line
660	  bundle \(\mathcal O_C(D)\) and its cohomology are constructed; once
661	  \texttt{RR.3} is closed, the present theorem inherits the
662	  vanishing as an instance, and the proof closes verbatim as
663	  displayed. Pending \texttt{RR.3}, the Lean signature
664	  \texttt{l\_eq\_degree\_plus\_one\_of\_genus\_zero} threads the
665	  \(H^1\)-vanishing hypothesis explicitly as a named premise; the
666	  iteration that closes \texttt{RR.3} then drops the premise in a
667	  follow-up signature simplification.
668	
669	  \emph{Sub-build note for \texttt{RR.4}.} The downstream consumer of
670	  this theorem is the genus-\(0\) classification
671	  REF of
672	  \texttt{AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex}: with \(\ell(D) = \deg(D) + 1\)
673	  and the degree-zero principal-divisor identity
674	  (REF), Hartshorne's Example~1.3.5 chain
675	  produces a degree-\(1\) morphism \(C \to \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) via
676	  \texttt{Proj.fromOfGlobalSections} applied to the two-dimensional
677	  \(H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P]))\), which is then upgraded to an
678	  isomorphism in \texttt{RR.4}.
679	\end{proof}
680	
681	\section{Out of scope}
682	
683	The following items are deliberately \emph{not} part of \texttt{RR.2}
684	and are sequenced into the sibling sub-build chapters:
685	
686	\begin{itemize}
687	  \item The general-\(g\) Riemann--Roch theorem
688	    \(\ell(D) - \ell(K - D) = \deg(D) + 1 - g\) (Hartshorne~IV.1.3 in
689	    its full form). This requires the canonical divisor \(K\), the
690	    canonical sheaf \(\Omega^1_{C / \bar k} = \omega_C\), and
691	    Serre duality $H^1(C, \mathcal L) \cong H^0(C, \omega_C \otimes
692	    \mathcal L^{-1})^*$. Mathlib has none of these for schemes, and
693	    the genus-\(0\) critical path does not require them; the general
694	    statement is queued for a follow-up chapter (post-RR-bridge).
695	  \item The construction of the invertible sheaf \(\mathcal O_C(D)\)
696	    from a Weil divisor \(D\), the linear-equivalence isomorphism
697	    \(\mathcal O_C(D) \cong \mathcal O_C(D')\) when \(D \sim D'\), and
698	    the computation of $H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P])) \cong \bar k \cdot
699	    1 \oplus \bar k \cdot t$ (\emph{a non-constant rational function
700	    \(t\) exists}) live in \texttt{RR.3} (sibling chapter
701	    \texttt{RiemannRoch\_OcOfD.tex}, to be added). Inside the proof
702	    of REF the dependency on
703	    \(\mathcal O_C(D)\) is reduced to its \(H^0\) functor and the short
704	    exact sequence of the closed point.
705	  \item The vanishing \(H^1(C, \mathcal O_C(D)) = 0\) for
706	    \(\deg(D) \geq 0\) on a genus-\(0\) curve is consumed as a named
707	    premise by REF; its closure is
708	    part of \texttt{RR.3} (where the cohomology of
709	    \(\mathcal O_{\mathbb P^1}(d)\) is computed) and is recovered for
710	    a general smooth proper genus-\(0\) curve via the isomorphism
711	    \(C \cong \mathbb P^1\) of \texttt{RR.4} or directly via the
712	    base-change argument inside \texttt{RR.3}.
713	  \item The classification ``a smooth proper geometrically irreducible
714	    curve over \(\bar k\) with \(g = 0\) is isomorphic to
715	    \(\mathbb P^1\)'' (Hartshorne Example~IV.1.3.5 proper) is the
716	    headline of \texttt{RR.4} (sibling chapter
717	    \texttt{RiemannRoch\_RationalIsoP1.tex}, to be added). The
718	    present chapter supplies one of its two inputs (the $\ell(D) =
719	    \deg(D) + 1\( identity); the other input is the degree-\)0$
720	    principal-divisor identity REF from
721	    \texttt{RR.1}.
722	  \item Higher-cohomology bookkeeping (\(\chi\) as an alternating sum
723	    over all \(i \geq 0\), the Grothendieck vanishing
724	    \(H^i(C, \mathcal F) = 0\) for \(i > \dim C = 1\)) is not separately
725	    formalised here; the chapter consumes only the two-term form
726	    \(\chi(\mathcal F) = \dim H^0 - \dim H^1\) which is the standing
727	    curve specialisation.
728	\end{itemize}

434	constant sheaf at `X`) and `(X ⟶ (Sheaf.Γ).obj F)` (module morphisms into the

446	noncomputable def constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv
447	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C]
448	    (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
449	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
450	    (X : ModuleCat.{u} k) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
451	    ((constantSheaf J _).obj X ⟶ F) ≃ₗ[k] (X ⟶ (Sheaf.Γ J _).obj F) :=
452	  haveI : (presheafToSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k :=
453	    (sheafificationAdjunction J _).left_adjoint_linear k
454	  haveI : (constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Additive := by
455	    unfold constantSheaf; infer_instance
456	  haveI : (Sheaf.Γ J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Additive :=
457	    (constantSheafΓAdj J _).right_adjoint_additive
458	  haveI : (constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k := by
459	    unfold constantSheaf; infer_instance
460	  haveI : (Sheaf.Γ J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k :=
461	    (constantSheafΓAdj J _).right_adjoint_linear k
462	  (constantSheafΓAdj J _).homLinearEquiv k X F

464	/-- Iter-046: Hom-from-`k` upgrade. The Hom group `(ModuleCat.of k k ⟶ M)` for
465	`M : ModuleCat k` is canonically `k`-LinearEquivalent to `M` via `f ↦ f 1`.
466	Direct one-liner combining `ModuleCat.homLinearEquiv` (Mathlib's
467	LinearEquiv-version of the underlying-LinearMap correspondence) with
468	`LinearMap.ringLmapEquivSelf` (the standard `(R →ₗ[R] M) ≃ₗ[S] M` evaluation). -/
469	noncomputable def homFromOne_linearEquiv (k : Type u) [Field k] (M : ModuleCat.{u} k) :
470	    (ModuleCat.of k k ⟶ M) ≃ₗ[k] M :=
471	  (ModuleCat.homLinearEquiv (M := ModuleCat.of k k) (N := M) (S := k)).trans
472	    (LinearMap.ringLmapEquivSelf k k M)

55	/-- A skyscraper presheaf is a presheaf supported at a single point: if `p₀ ∈ X` is a specified
56	point, then the skyscraper presheaf `𝓕` with value `A` is defined by `U ↦ A` if `p₀ ∈ U` and
57	`U ↦ *` if `p₀ ∉ A` where `*` is some terminal object.
58	-/
59	@[simps]
60	def skyscraperPresheaf : Presheaf C X where
61	  obj U := if p₀ ∈ unop U then A else terminal C
62	  map {U V} i :=
63	    if h : p₀ ∈ unop V then eqToHom <| by rw [if_pos h, if_pos (by simpa using i.unop.le h)]
64	    else ((if_neg h).symm.ndrec terminalIsTerminal).from _
65	  map_id U :=
66	    (em (p₀ ∈ U.unop)).elim (fun h => dif_pos h) fun h =>
67	      ((if_neg h).symm.ndrec terminalIsTerminal).hom_ext _ _
68	  map_comp {U V W} iVU iWV := by
69	    by_cases hW : p₀ ∈ unop W
70	    · have hV : p₀ ∈ unop V := leOfHom iWV.unop hW
71	      simp only [dif_pos hW, dif_pos hV, eqToHom_trans]
72	    · dsimp; rw [dif_neg hW]; apply ((if_neg hW).symm.ndrec terminalIsTerminal).hom_ext
73	
74	theorem skyscraperPresheaf_eq_pushforward
75	    [hd : ∀ U : Opens (TopCat.of PUnit.{u + 1}), Decidable (PUnit.unit ∈ U)] :
76	    skyscraperPresheaf p₀ A =
77	      (ofHom (ContinuousMap.const (TopCat.of PUnit) p₀)) _*
78	        skyscraperPresheaf (X := TopCat.of PUnit) PUnit.unit A := by
79	  convert_to @skyscraperPresheaf X p₀ (fun U => hd <| (Opens.map <| ofHom <|
80	      ContinuousMap.const _ p₀).obj U)
81	    C _ _ A = _ <;> congr

83	/-- Taking skyscraper presheaf at a point is functorial: `c ↦ skyscraper p₀ c` defines a functor by
84	sending every `f : a ⟶ b` to the natural transformation `α` defined as: `α(U) = f : a ⟶ b` if
85	`p₀ ∈ U` and the unique morphism to a terminal object in `C` if `p₀ ∉ U`.
86	-/
87	@[simps]
88	def SkyscraperPresheafFunctor.map' {a b : C} (f : a ⟶ b) :
89	    skyscraperPresheaf p₀ a ⟶ skyscraperPresheaf p₀ b where
90	  app U :=
91	    if h : p₀ ∈ U.unop then eqToHom (if_pos h) ≫ f ≫ eqToHom (if_pos h).symm
92	    else ((if_neg h).symm.ndrec terminalIsTerminal).from _
93	  naturality U V i := by
94	    simp only [skyscraperPresheaf_map]
95	    by_cases hV : p₀ ∈ V.unop
96	    · have hU : p₀ ∈ U.unop := leOfHom i.unop hV
97	      simp only [skyscraperPresheaf_obj, hU, hV, ↓reduceDIte, eqToHom_trans_assoc, Category.assoc,
98	        eqToHom_trans]
99	    · apply ((if_neg hV).symm.ndrec terminalIsTerminal).hom_ext
100	
101	theorem SkyscraperPresheafFunctor.map'_id {a : C} :
102	    SkyscraperPresheafFunctor.map' p₀ (𝟙 a) = 𝟙 _ := by
103	  ext U
104	  simp only [SkyscraperPresheafFunctor.map'_app]; split_ifs <;> cat_disch
105	
106	theorem SkyscraperPresheafFunctor.map'_comp {a b c : C} (f : a ⟶ b) (g : b ⟶ c) :
107	    SkyscraperPresheafFunctor.map' p₀ (f ≫ g) =
108	      SkyscraperPresheafFunctor.map' p₀ f ≫ SkyscraperPresheafFunctor.map' p₀ g := by
109	  ext U
110	  simp only [SkyscraperPresheafFunctor.map'_app]
111	  split_ifs with h <;> cat_disch
112	
113	/-- Taking skyscraper presheaf at a point is functorial: `c ↦ skyscraper p₀ c` defines a functor by
114	sending every `f : a ⟶ b` to the natural transformation `α` defined as: `α(U) = f : a ⟶ b` if
115	`p₀ ∈ U` and the unique morphism to a terminal object in `C` if `p₀ ∉ U`.
116	-/
117	@[simps]
118	def skyscraperPresheafFunctor : C ⥤ Presheaf C X where
119	  obj := skyscraperPresheaf p₀
120	  map := SkyscraperPresheafFunctor.map' p₀
121	  map_id _ := SkyscraperPresheafFunctor.map'_id p₀
122	  map_comp := SkyscraperPresheafFunctor.map'_comp p₀

246	/-- Taking skyscraper sheaf at a point is functorial: `c ↦ skyscraper p₀ c` defines a functor by

261	defined by: `𝓕.germ p₀ ≫ f : 𝓕(U) ⟶ c` if `p₀ ∈ U` and the unique morphism to a terminal object
262	if `p₀ ∉ U`.

265	def toSkyscraperPresheaf {𝓕 : Presheaf C X} {c : C} (f : 𝓕.stalk p₀ ⟶ c) :
266	    𝓕 ⟶ skyscraperPresheaf p₀ c where

269	    else ((if_neg h).symm.ndrec terminalIsTerminal).from _

273	    · have hU : p₀ ∈ U.unop := leOfHom inc.unop hV
274	      split_ifs

293	          simp only [dif_pos hV] }

12	/-!
13	# Skyscraper (pre)sheaves
14	
15	A skyscraper (pre)sheaf `𝓕 : (Pre)Sheaf C X` is the (pre)sheaf with value `A` at point `p₀` that is
16	supported only at open sets contain `p₀`, i.e. `𝓕(U) = A` if `p₀ ∈ U` and `𝓕(U) = *` if `p₀ ∉ U`
17	where `*` is a terminal object of `C`. In terms of stalks, `𝓕` is supported at all specializations
18	of `p₀`, i.e. if `p₀ ⤳ x` then `𝓕ₓ ≅ A` and if `¬ p₀ ⤳ x` then `𝓕ₓ ≅ *`.
19	
20	## Main definitions
21	
22	* `skyscraperPresheaf`: `skyscraperPresheaf p₀ A` is the skyscraper presheaf at point `p₀` with
23	  value `A`.
24	* `skyscraperSheaf`: the skyscraper presheaf satisfies the sheaf condition.
25	
26	## Main statements
27	
28	* `skyscraperPresheafStalkOfSpecializes`: if `y ∈ closure {p₀}` then the stalk of
29	  `skyscraperPresheaf p₀ A` at `y` is `A`.
30	* `skyscraperPresheafStalkOfNotSpecializes`: if `y ∉ closure {p₀}` then the stalk of
31	  `skyscraperPresheaf p₀ A` at `y` is `*` the terminal object.
32	
33	TODO: generalize universe level when calculating stalks, after generalizing universe level of stalk.
34	TODO(@joelriou): refactor the definitions in this file so as to make them
35	particular cases of general constructions for points of sites from
36	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Point/Skyscraper.lean`.
37	
38	-/

/-- **H⁰ identification for a closed-point skyscraper** (Lane H sub-helper —
iter-188).

On a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C / kbar`, the global
sections of the closed-point skyscraper sheaf
`skyscraperSheaf P.point (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)` form a one-dimensional
`kbar`-vector space:
`dim_{kbar} H⁰(C, k(P)) = 1`.

The proof composes the project's standard four-step `kbar`-linear-equivalence
chain — `HModule_zero_linearEquiv` (Ext₀ ≃ Hom-from-constant-sheaf) +
`constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv` (constant-sheaf/Γ adjunction) +
`homFromOne_linearEquiv` (Hom-from-`k` evaluation at `1`) +
`SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top` (Γ reads the top section) — to identify
`HModule kbar (skyscraperSheaf P.point _) 0` with the underlying presheaf
evaluated at the top open `⊤`. Since `P.point ∈ ⊤` (trivially) the
skyscraper-presheaf evaluates at `⊤` to its value `ModuleCat.of kbar kbar`,
whose `kbar`-finrank is `1` by `Module.finrank_self`. -/
private theorem Scheme.H0_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_one
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
    (P : C.left.PrimeDivisor)
    [∀ (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left), Decidable (P.point ∈ U)] :
    Module.finrank kbar
        (Scheme.HModule kbar
          (skyscraperSheaf (C := ModuleCat.{u} kbar) P.point
            (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)) 0) = 1 := by
  -- The four-step `kbar`-linear-equivalence chain identifying H⁰ with the
  -- presheaf evaluation at the top open.
  let F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) :=
    skyscraperSheaf (C := ModuleCat.{u} kbar) P.point (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)
  let LE1 := Scheme.HModule_zero_linearEquiv kbar F
  let LE2 :=
    AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv kbar
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar) F
  let LE3 :=
    AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.homFromOne_linearEquiv kbar
      ((Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
        (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)).obj F)
  let LE4 :=
    AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top kbar F
  let LE := ((LE1.trans LE2).trans LE3).trans LE4
  rw [LE.finrank_eq]
  -- Goal: Module.finrank kbar (F.obj.obj (op ⊤)) = 1.
  -- Reduce the skyscraper-presheaf evaluation at ⊤: since P.point ∈ ⊤,
  -- the dite branch picks the value `ModuleCat.of kbar kbar`.
  show Module.finrank kbar
    ((skyscraperPresheaf (C := ModuleCat.{u} kbar) P.point
      (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)).obj
      (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))) = 1
  rw [skyscraperPresheaf_obj]
  rw [if_pos (show P.point ∈ (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) from
    trivial)]
  exact Module.finrank_self kbar

/-- **H¹ vanishing for a closed-point skyscraper** (Lane H sub-helper —
iter-188).

On a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C / kbar`, the first
cohomology of the closed-point skyscraper sheaf vanishes:
`dim_{kbar} H¹(C, k(P)) = 0`.

The mathematical content: the closed-point skyscraper sheaf is the
pushforward of a sheaf from a one-point space (Hartshorne~III.2.5(c)),
hence flasque, and flasque sheaves have vanishing higher cohomology on
any topological space (Hartshorne~III.2.5).

**iter-188 Lane H status** — Tier-3 honest typed sorry. The body waits
on the project-side `ModuleCat kbar`-flavoured flasque-cohomology bridge
(or upstream Mathlib `Sheaf.flasque_vanishing_cohomology` for
`Abelian.Ext`-cohomology sheaves with values in an abelian category with
enough injectives). The committed `RR.2.H¹` sub-phase of STRATEGY.md
schedules this body (~150-300 LOC across ~8-12 iters; off the iter-188
H⁰ critical-path target). -/
private theorem Scheme.H1_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_zero
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
    (P : C.left.PrimeDivisor)
    [∀ (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left), Decidable (P.point ∈ U)] :
    Module.finrank kbar
        (Scheme.HModule kbar
          (skyscraperSheaf (C := ModuleCat.{u} kbar) P.point
            (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)) 1) = 0 := by
  sorry

private theorem Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_skyscraperSheaf
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
    (P : C.left.PrimeDivisor)
    [∀ (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left), Decidable (P.point ∈ U)] :
    Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
        (skyscraperSheaf (C := ModuleCat.{u} kbar) P.point
          (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)) = 1 := by
  -- Unfold χ = dim H⁰ − dim H¹ and substitute the two halves.
  unfold Scheme.eulerCharacteristic
  rw [Scheme.H0_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_one C P,
    Scheme.H1_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_zero C P]
  -- Arithmetic: (1 : ℤ) − (0 : ℤ) = 1.
  simp

/-- **χ of the closed-point skyscraper is 1** (Lane H sub-helper —
iter-186).

On a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C / kbar`, the
Euler characteristic of the closed-point skyscraper sheaf
`skyscraperSheaf P.point (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)` is `1`:
`χ(k(P)) = dim H⁰(C, k(P)) − dim H¹(C, k(P)) = 1 − 0 = 1`.

**Proof outline (queued infrastructure).** The H⁰ identification
`H⁰(C, skyscraperSheaf P kbar) ≅ kbar` follows from the
stalk-skyscraper adjunction (Mathlib `stalkSkyscraperSheafAdjunction`)
combined with the global-sections-as-Hom-from-constant-sheaf bridge
(project-side `HModule_zero_linearEquiv` chain). The H¹ vanishing
follows from the flasqueness of a skyscraper sheaf (Hartshorne III.2.5,
or Mathlib `flasque_skyscraperSheaf`-style lemma; pending project-side
bridge).

**iter-186 Lane H status** — Tier-3 honest typed sorry. The body
depends on the project-side `ModuleCat kbar`-flavoured skyscraper
cohomology API (currently absent at Mathlib `b80f227`). -/
/-- **H⁰ identification for a closed-point skyscraper** (Lane H sub-helper —
iter-188).

On a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C / kbar`, the global
sections of the closed-point skyscraper sheaf
`skyscraperSheaf P.point (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)` form a one-dimensional
`kbar`-vector space:
`dim_{kbar} H⁰(C, k(P)) = 1`.

The proof composes the project's standard four-step `kbar`-linear-equivalence
chain — `HModule_zero_linearEquiv` (Ext₀ ≃ Hom-from-constant-sheaf) +
`constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv` (constant-sheaf/Γ adjunction) +
`homFromOne_linearEquiv` (Hom-from-`k` evaluation at `1`) +
`SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top` (Γ reads the top section) — to identify
`HModule kbar (skyscraperSheaf P.point _) 0` with the underlying presheaf
evaluated at the top open `⊤`. Since `P.point ∈ ⊤` (trivially) the
skyscraper-presheaf evaluates at `⊤` to its value `ModuleCat.of kbar kbar`,
whose `kbar`-finrank is `1` by `Module.finrank_self`. -/
private theorem Scheme.H0_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_one
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
    (P : C.left.PrimeDivisor)
    [∀ (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left), Decidable (P.point ∈ U)] :
    Module.finrank kbar
        (Scheme.HModule kbar
          (skyscraperSheaf (C := ModuleCat.{u} kbar) P.point
            (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)) 0) = 1 := by
  -- The four-step `kbar`-linear-equivalence chain identifying H⁰ with the
  -- presheaf evaluation at the top open.
  let F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) :=
    skyscraperSheaf (C := ModuleCat.{u} kbar) P.point (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)
  let LE1 := Scheme.HModule_zero_linearEquiv kbar F
  let LE2 :=
    AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv kbar
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar) F
  let LE3 :=
    AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.homFromOne_linearEquiv kbar
      ((Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
        (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)).obj F)
  let LE4 :=
    AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top kbar F
  let LE := ((LE1.trans LE2).trans LE3).trans LE4
  rw [LE.finrank_eq]
  -- Goal: Module.finrank kbar (F.obj.obj (op ⊤)) = 1.
  -- Reduce the skyscraper-presheaf evaluation at ⊤: since P.point ∈ ⊤,
  -- the dite branch picks the value `ModuleCat.of kbar kbar`.
  show Module.finrank kbar
    ((skyscraperPresheaf (C := ModuleCat.{u} kbar) P.point
      (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)).obj
      (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))) = 1
  rw [skyscraperPresheaf_obj]
  rw [if_pos (show P.point ∈ (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) from
    trivial)]
  exact Module.finrank_self kbar

/-- **H¹ vanishing for a closed-point skyscraper** (Lane H sub-helper —
iter-188).

On a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C / kbar`, the first
cohomology of the closed-point skyscraper sheaf vanishes:
`dim_{kbar} H¹(C, k(P)) = 0`.

The mathematical content: the closed-point skyscraper sheaf is the
pushforward of a sheaf from a one-point space (Hartshorne~III.2.5(c)),
hence flasque, and flasque sheaves have vanishing higher cohomology on
any topological space (Hartshorne~III.2.5).

**iter-188 Lane H status** — Tier-3 honest typed sorry. The body waits
on the project-side `ModuleCat kbar`-flavoured flasque-cohomology bridge
(or upstream Mathlib `Sheaf.flasque_vanishing_cohomology` for
`Abelian.Ext`-cohomology sheaves with values in an abelian category with
enough injectives). The committed `RR.2.H¹` sub-phase of STRATEGY.md
schedules this body (~150-300 LOC across ~8-12 iters; off the iter-188
H⁰ critical-path target). -/
private theorem Scheme.H1_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_zero
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
    (P : C.left.PrimeDivisor)
    [∀ (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left), Decidable (P.point ∈ U)] :
    Module.finrank kbar
        (Scheme.HModule kbar
          (skyscraperSheaf (C := ModuleCat.{u} kbar) P.point
            (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)) 1) = 0 := by
  sorry

private theorem Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_skyscraperSheaf
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
    (P : C.left.PrimeDivisor)
    [∀ (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left), Decidable (P.point ∈ U)] :
    Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
        (skyscraperSheaf (C := ModuleCat.{u} kbar) P.point
          (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)) = 1 := by
  -- Unfold χ = dim H⁰ − dim H¹ and substitute the two halves.
  unfold Scheme.eulerCharacteristic
  rw [Scheme.H0_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_one C P,
    Scheme.H1_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_zero C P]
  -- Arithmetic: (1 : ℤ) − (0 : ℤ) = 1.
  simp

iter-188).

On a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C / kbar`, the global
sections of the closed-point skyscraper sheaf
`skyscraperSheaf P.point (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)` form a one-dimensional

`dim_{kbar} H⁰(C, k(P)) = 1`.

The proof composes the project's standard four-step `kbar`-linear-equivalence
chain — `HModule_zero_linearEquiv` (Ext₀ ≃ Hom-from-constant-sheaf) +
`constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv` (constant-sheaf/Γ adjunction) +
`homFromOne_linearEquiv` (Hom-from-`k` evaluation at `1`) +
`SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top` (Γ reads the top section) — to identify
`HModule kbar (skyscraperSheaf P.point _) 0` with the underlying presheaf
evaluated at the top open `⊤`. Since `P.point ∈ ⊤` (trivially) the
skyscraper-presheaf evaluates at `⊤` to its value `ModuleCat.of kbar kbar`,
whose `kbar`-finrank is `1` by `Module.finrank_self`. -/
private theorem Scheme.H0_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_one
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
    (P : C.left.PrimeDivisor)
    [∀ (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left), Decidable (P.point ∈ U)] :
    Module.finrank kbar
        (Scheme.HModule kbar
          (skyscraperSheaf (C := ModuleCat.{u} kbar) P.point
            (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)) 0) = 1 := by
  -- The four-step `kbar`-linear-equivalence chain identifying H⁰ with the
  -- presheaf evaluation at the top open (mirrors the structure-sheaf chain
  -- of `finrank_H0_toModuleKSheaf_eq_one`).
  let LE1 :=
    Scheme.HModule_zero_linearEquiv kbar
      (skyscraperSheaf (C := ModuleCat.{u} kbar) P.point
        (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar))
  let LE2 :=
    AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv kbar
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)
      (skyscraperSheaf (C := ModuleCat.{u} kbar) P.point
        (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar))
  let LE3 :=
    AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.homFromOne_linearEquiv kbar
      ((Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
        (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)).obj
        (skyscraperSheaf (C := ModuleCat.{u} kbar) P.point
          (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)))
  let LE4 :=
    AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top kbar
      (skyscraperSheaf (C := ModuleCat.{u} kbar) P.point
        (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar))
  let LE := ((LE1.trans LE2).trans LE3).trans LE4
  rw [LE.finrank_eq]
  -- Goal: Module.finrank kbar ((skyscraperSheaf ...).obj.obj (op ⊤)) = 1.
  -- Reduce the skyscraper-presheaf evaluation at ⊤: since P.point ∈ ⊤,
  -- the dite branch picks the value `ModuleCat.of kbar kbar`.
  change Module.finrank kbar
    ((skyscraperPresheaf (C := ModuleCat.{u} kbar) P.point
      (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)).obj
      (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))) = 1
  rw [skyscraperPresheaf_obj,
    if_pos (show P.point ∈ (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) from
      trivial)]
  exact Module.finrank_self kbar

# AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RRFormula.lean

## Iter-188 Lane H — HARD BAR ≥1 axiom-clean close

**Verdict: HARD BAR met.** New axiom-clean helper
`Scheme.H0_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_one` lands the H⁰ half of
`eulerCharacteristic_skyscraperSheaf`; the H¹ half is factored into a
focused named typed-sorry helper `Scheme.H1_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_zero`
(per blueprint Tier-3 status / `RR.2.H¹` STRATEGY.md sub-phase). The parent
`Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_skyscraperSheaf` body is now sorry-free
modulo the H¹ helper.

### Net change
- Sorry count in file: **2 → 2** (no net change — H¹ blocker localised
  into a focused helper; parent body axiom-clean assembly).
- Sorries: L322 (`eulerCharacteristic_shortExact_add`, pre-existing —
  off-target) + L468 (`H1_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_zero`, new narrow
  typed-sorry replacing the parent's bare `sorry`).
- `eulerCharacteristic_skyscraperSheaf` body: bare `sorry` → axiom-clean
  assembly modulo two named helpers.

### Declarations added (iter-188)

#### `Scheme.H0_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_one` (L423) — **AXIOM-CLEAN** ✅
Conclusion:
`Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar (skyscraperSheaf P.point (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)) 0) = 1`.

Verified axioms (via `lean_verify`):
`propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound` — only kernel axioms; no
`sorryAx`.

Proof body (~45 LOC) follows the blueprint's three-step chain (mirrors
the structure-sheaf `finrank_H0_toModuleKSheaf_eq_one` recipe):
1. `Scheme.HModule_zero_linearEquiv kbar F` —
   `HModule kbar F 0 ≃ₗ[kbar] ((constantSheaf J _).obj (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar) ⟶ F)`.
2. `Scheme.constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv kbar J (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar) F` —
   `(constSheaf k ⟶ F) ≃ₗ[kbar] (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar ⟶ Sheaf.Γ.obj F)`.
3. `Scheme.homFromOne_linearEquiv kbar (Sheaf.Γ.obj F)` —
   `(k ⟶ Γ.obj F) ≃ₗ[kbar] Γ.obj F`.
4. `Scheme.SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top kbar F` —
   `Γ.obj F ≃ₗ[kbar] F.obj.obj (op ⊤)`.

After `rw [LE.finrank_eq]` the goal reduces to
`Module.finrank kbar (F.obj.obj (op ⊤)) = 1`. A `change` exposes the
skyscraper-presheaf evaluation. The dite branch picks
`ModuleCat.of kbar kbar` via `if_pos trivial` (since `P.point ∈ ⊤`
trivially), and `Module.finrank_self kbar` closes.

#### `Scheme.H1_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_zero` (L468) — Tier-3 typed sorry
Conclusion:
`Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar (skyscraperSheaf P.point (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)) 1) = 0`.

Body: `sorry`. Closure depends on the project-side `ModuleCat kbar`-
flavoured flasque-cohomology bridge (the committed `RR.2.H¹` STRATEGY.md
sub-phase, ~150-300 LOC across ~8-12 iters; off the iter-188 H⁰ critical
path).

#### `Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_skyscraperSheaf` (L494) — sorry-free body
Conclusion (unchanged):
`Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C (skyscraperSheaf P.point (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)) = 1`.

Proof body (~15 LOC): `unfold eulerCharacteristic` → `change` (with
explicit `(C := ModuleCat.{u} kbar)` annotation needed to normalise
the goal so `Module.finrank` patterns under `↑(·)` casts unify with the
helper conclusions) → `rw [H0_..., H1_...]` → `simp` (closes `1 - 0 = 1`).

### Implementation notes / dead-ends

#### Attempt 1 — direct `rw` without normalisation `change`
- Approach: `unfold eulerCharacteristic; rw [Scheme.H0_..., Scheme.H1_...]; simp`.
- Result: **FAILED**. `rw` reports "Did not find an occurrence of the
  pattern `Module.finrank kbar (HModule kbar (skyscraperSheaf ...) 0)`
  in the target `↑(Module.finrank kbar (HModule kbar (skyscraperSheaf ...) 0)) - ...`",
  despite the pattern being syntactically present inside the `↑(·)`
  cast.
- Diagnosis: instance/universe inference for the implicit `(C := ?M)`
  argument of `skyscraperSheaf` differs between the helper-conclusion's
  elaboration and the parent's elaboration — even `simp only [h0, h1]`
  with explicit hypotheses had `made no progress`, and `omega` /
  `linarith` could not bridge the cast-wrapped expressions.

#### Attempt 2 — `change` to normalise (RESOLVED)
- Approach: prepend `change ((Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar
  (skyscraperSheaf (C := ModuleCat.{u} kbar) P.point (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)) 0) : ℕ) : ℤ) - ...`
  to force Lean to elaborate the implicit universes consistently with
  the helper conclusions.
- Result: **RESOLVED**. After the `change`, both `rw [H0_...]` and
  `rw [H1_...]` succeed and `simp` closes the arithmetic residual.

#### Attempt 3 — `simp [skyscraperPresheaf_obj]` / `simp only [if_true]` for the H0 helper
- After `rw [skyscraperPresheaf_obj]` the goal becomes
  `Module.finrank kbar ↑(if P.point ∈ Opposite.unop (Opposite.op ⊤) then ModuleCat.of kbar kbar else ⊤_ ModuleCat kbar) = 1`.
- `simp only [Opposite.unop_op, if_pos (P.point ∈ ⊤)]` only fired
  the `unop_op` step; `if_pos` was reported as unused.
- `rw [if_pos trivial]` failed: pattern needs `if True then ...` but
  the dite predicate is `P.point ∈ ⊤` (not `True`).
- **RESOLVED** via `exact (if_pos trivial).symm ▸ Module.finrank_self kbar`
  — the `▸` substitution lets Lean unify both the carrier and the dite
  predicate against the goal in a single shot.

### Blueprint sync (for review agent / sync_leanok)

Both new lemmas (H0 + H1) and the parent `eulerCharacteristic_skyscraperSheaf`
are pinned in `blueprint/src/chapters/RiemannRoch_RRFormula.tex`
(lem:euler_char_skyscraperSheaf already present iter-187). The blueprint's
two-paragraph proof body explicitly factors the H⁰ closure and the
H¹ gating, matching the new helper split. The `sync_leanok` phase
should:
- Confirm `lem:euler_char_skyscraperSheaf` (`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_skyscraperSheaf`)
  retains `\leanok` if its body is sorry-free (it is now, modulo
  `H1_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_zero` via the sorryAx transitivity
  rule — `\leanok` is for sorry-free *bodies*, so verify against the
  project convention).

The blueprint does not currently pin the two new helpers
(`H0_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_one`, `H1_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_zero`)
as separate Lean declarations — they are factored sub-lemmas of the
single chapter lemma `lem:euler_char_skyscraperSheaf`. The plan agent
may consider whether to pin them as separate substrate lemmas in a
future iter (low priority; they are private and the blueprint already
describes the split in the proof body).

### Active monitors (per PROGRESS.md)

- **Lane H HARD BAR**: H⁰ half closure was the iter-188 target.
  **STATUS: met (axiom-clean).** H¹ half remains gated on `RR.2.H¹`
  STRATEGY sub-phase (no iter-188 prover work; iter-189+).
- **`eulerCharacteristic_shortExact_add` (L322)**: unchanged; still
  the LES-carrier-substrate gating (off-target this iter).

### Iter-189 follow-ups

1. **`RR.2.H¹` skeleton** (per STRATEGY.md): begin project-side flasque
   cohomology bridge to close `H1_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_zero`.
2. **`eulerCharacteristic_shortExact_add` body** (L322): begin LES-carrier
   substrate work — `ModuleCat kbar`-flavoured `Abelian.Ext.covariantSequence`
   bridge + Grothendieck vanishing for `HModule kbar _ i ≥ 2` + 6-term
   rank counting (separate from the H¹ flasque sub-phase).
3. With both `eulerCharacteristic_shortExact_add` and
   `H1_skyscraperSheaf_finrank_eq_zero` closed,
   `eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_succ` (L515) and the main
   `eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus` (L633) inherit
   axiom-cleanness modulo only `Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf` (the `RR.3`
   typed sorry).

11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. **Requires `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in env** — check via `env | grep -E "OPENAI|GEMINI|OPENROUTER"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | — | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/` (`prover-autoformalize.md` / `prover-prover.md` / `prover-polish.md`).
127	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
128	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
129	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
130	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
131	
132	### Subagents
133	
134	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
135	
136	### Review Agent
137	
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
139	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
140	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
141	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
142	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	These run automatically between phases:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
150	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
151	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
152

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before stopping:
130	
131	1. Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with: current result, lemmas discovered, concrete next step, dead-end warnings.
132	2. Save all changes; ensure the file compiles (use scoped `sorry` for stuck parts if needed).
133

10	/-!
11	# The rational-curve isomorphism `C ≅ ℙ¹` (RR.4)
12	
13	This file is the **RR.4** sub-build chapter for the project's headline
14	`genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (the "smooth proper geometrically irreducible
15	genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is isomorphic to `ℙ¹`" lemma pinned in
16	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity:290`).
17	
18	The Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain for the genus-`0` ⇒ `ℙ¹` classification routes
19	through the four sub-build chapters:
20	
21	- `RR.1` (`WeilDivisor.lean`): the Weil divisor group `Div(C)`, the degree
22	  map `deg : Div(C) → ℤ`, and the degree-zero principal-divisor identity.
23	- `RR.2` (`RRFormula.lean`): the Euler-characteristic identity
24	  `χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g` and Riemann–Roch in genus zero.
25	- `RR.3` (`OCofP.lean`): the line bundle `𝒪_C([P])` of a closed point and
26	  the existence of a non-constant rational function
27	  `f ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C([P])) ∖ k̄ · 1`.
28	- **`RR.4` (this file)**: the global classification — the linear-system
29	  morphism `C ⟶ ℙ¹` produced from `(1, f)` (Pin 1), its degree identified
30	  via the pole divisor (Pin 2), and the degree-`1` ⇒ isomorphism step
31	  (Pin 3). The headline target `AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero_curve_iso_P1`
32	  (Pin 4) is the existing pinned declaration at
33	  `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean:290` — see §0 below.
34	
35	## Status (iter-177 file-skeleton; iter-181 Pin 3 signature refinement)
36	
37	This file was originally the **iter-177 Lane 8** file-skeleton. Each
38	of the three new pinned declarations carries the *intended*
39	substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
40	`blueprint/src/chapters/RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso.tex`) with a
41	`sorry` body. Bodies are iter-182+ work after the RR.1/RR.2/RR.3
42	chain matures (in particular `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_degree_zero`
43	and the `H⁰(C, 𝒪_C([P]))`-non-constant existence corollary of `RR.3`).
44	
45	iter-181 Lane I refined the Pin 3 signature of
46	`iso_of_degree_one`: the abstract function-field-iso existence
47	hypothesis is replaced by the `[Algebra K(C') K(C)]` typeclass binder
48	+ `Module.finrank K(C') K(C) = 1` equation, matching what the
49	iter-182+ body needs (per `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md`
50	Decision 2 DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY).
51	
52	The 3 new pinned declarations are:
53	
54	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphismToP1OfGlobalSections` — from a
55	   graded `k̄`-algebra hom `k̄[X₀, X₁] →+* Γ(X, ⊤)` whose image of the
56	   irrelevant ideal generates the unit ideal, produce the morphism
57	   `X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` via `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` lifted to
58	   the slice category over `Spec k̄`.
59	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor` — for a
60	   non-constant morphism `φ : C ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` from a smooth
61	   proper curve, extract a positive-degree Weil divisor on `C` (the
62	   pole divisor `φ^*[∞]`).
63	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.iso_of_degree_one` — a non-constant
64	   morphism between smooth proper geometrically irreducible curves
65	   whose induced function-field map is a `k̄`-algebra iso is an
66	   isomorphism of schemes (Hartshorne I.6.12 specialised to
67	   degree-`1`).
68	
69	The headline pin (Pin 4) is satisfied by the existing AVR.lean target.
70	
71	## Note on type expressivity
72	
73	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
74	each pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
75	
76	* `morphismToP1OfGlobalSections` returns a *concrete* morphism
77	  `X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` of `Over (Spec k̄)`, not `Iso.refl _` or a
78	  reflexive placeholder; the input data is the exact pair
79	  `(f, hf)` consumed by Mathlib's `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`.
80	* `morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor` asserts the existence of a
81	  positive-integer degree `d` together with a Weil divisor `D` on `C`
82	  realising it — the *named substantive content* of the degree-via-pole
83	  identification. Unfolds to `∃ d D, 0 < d ∧ D.degree = d`, not `True`.
84	* `iso_of_degree_one` asserts `Nonempty (C ≅ C')` from the
85	  non-constant hypothesis together with the *substantive* degree-`1`
86	  hypothesis `Module.finrank C'.functionField C.functionField = 1`
87	  (paired with an `[Algebra C'.functionField C.functionField]` typeclass
88	  binder for the canonical `φ`-induced function-field map). The
89	  `Nonempty`-wrapped iso is the exact downstream-consumer shape,
90	  matching the `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` headline signature in AVR.lean.
91	  This signature was refined in iter-181 Lane I from the iter-177
92	  file-skeleton placeholder `Nonempty (C'.ff ≃+* C.ff)` to make the
93	  degree-`1`-induced-by-`φ` hypothesis the substantive content; see
94	  `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` Decision 2.
95	
96	Unfolding any pinned declaration exposes the named substantive content
97	(the concrete `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`-derived morphism, the
98	existence-of-positive-degree-divisor statement, the existence of a
99	scheme isomorphism); no `Iso.refl _` / empty-content `proof_wanted` /
100	`Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` placeholders are used.
101	
102	## Curve conventions
103	
104	Following the project (cf. `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`,
105	`Jacobian.lean`, `OCofP.lean`), a **smooth proper geometrically
106	irreducible curve over `k̄`** carries:
107	
108	- `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]`,
109	- `[IsProper C.hom]`,
110	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]`.
111	
112	These instances are supplied by `inferInstance` at the call site. The
113	`[IsIntegral C.left]` / `[IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]` instances
114	required by the Weil-divisor / `RationalMap.order` API are threaded
115	explicitly per-lemma where consumed.
